Includes the {{ill|Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|伊勢社}} and an Inari shrine. * {{ill|Fujimori Inari Shrine|ja|藤森稲荷神社}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社}} - Hosts the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival in November. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} - Located within the Local History Museum. The precinct also houses the Local History Museum (on the second floor of the shrine office) and Wakamiya Kindergarten. ==Cultural Properties== * '''Wakamiya Hachimangu Kagura:''' A type of {{ill|Hayashi Kagura|ja|囃子神楽}} performed on the grounds. Designated as a {{ill|Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|ja|川崎市地域文化財}} in November 2018.[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Decision on Kawasaki City’s First Regional Cultural Properties (Kawasaki City Board of Education)] ==Parish Districts== The shrine’s parish covers the following areas: * Former {{ill|Kawasaki Town|ja|川崎町}}: * Former Kuranezaki and Zōshiki Villages: {{ill|Suzuki Town|ja|鈴木町}} * Former Sunako Town: {{ill|Ekimoto Town|ja|駅前本町}} * Former {{ill|Daishi Town|ja|大師町}}: * Former Daishikawara Village: Ise Town, Kannon, Shōwa, Daishikawara, Dai Town, Fujisaki * Former Kawanakashima Village: Kawanakashima, Kannon, Daishi Ekimae, Daishi Honmachi, Daishi Town, Higashimonzen * Former Inarishinden: Egawa, {{ill|Kojima Town|ja|小島町}}, Shiohama, Shōwa, Tamachi, Dekino, {{ill|Tonomachi|ja|殿町}}, Hinode, Yotsuya Kami-machi, Yotsuya Shimo-machi * Former Ikegami Shinden: Ikegami Shin-machi, Ikegami Town * Former {{ill|Tajima Town|ja|田島町}}: * Former Ōshima Village: Asano Town * Former Kodachi Landfill: Ōkawa Town, Shiroishi Town * Reclaimed Land: {{ill|Ukishima Town|ja|浮島町}}, {{ill|Ogishima|ja|扇島}}, {{ill|Chidori Town|ja|千鳥町}}, Nakase, {{ill|Higashi Ogishima|ja|東扇島}}, Mizue Town, Yakō ==Lineage of Chief Priests== {| class="wikitable" ! Generation !! Chief Priest |- | First || Nakamura Kazuhiko |- | Second || Nakamura Hiroken |- | Third || Nakamura Hiroko |- | Fourth || Nakamura Hirohiko |- | Fifth || Nakamura Kimiko |- | Sixth || Nakamura Hiroyuki |} ==Associated Shrines== * {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|厳島神社}} (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * {{ill|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)|ja|稲荷神社}} (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * {{ill|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|神明神社}} (Kawanakashima and Shiohama). ==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi Station|ja|川崎大師駅}} on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス}} Kawasai 23 line. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==See Also== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachimangu (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮}} ==External Links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20051218123032/http://tomuraya.co.jp/wakamiya.htm Wakamiya Hachimangu Official Site] - Archived on January 27, 2020. * [https://www.facebook.com/wakamiyasama/ Wakamiya Hachimangu] - Facebook * [http://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/osirase/sigaku/shuhou/seido/meibo/kawasakiku.html Kawasaki City Religious Corporation Registry] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Guardian Shrines]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B7%9D%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]]

{{神社 | 名称=若宮八幡宮 | 画像=[[Image:Wakamiya Hachimangu 01.jpg|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|4.7|N|139|43|28.7|E}}}} | 所在地=神奈川県川崎市川崎区大師駅前2丁目13-16 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|32|4.78|N|139|43|28.79|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[仁徳天皇]] | 社格=旧[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]] | 創建=[[永禄]]2年（[[1559年]]）頃 | 例祭= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=32|lat_sec=4.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=43|lon_sec=28.7|label=若宮八幡宮|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} '''若宮八幡宮'''（わかみやはちまんぐう）は、[[神奈川県]][[川崎市]][[川崎区]][[大師駅前]]にある[[神社]]（[[若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)|若宮]][[八幡宮]]）である。旧[[大師町|大師河原]]総鎮守。境内社には「かなまら祭」で知られている[[金山神社 (川崎市)|金山神社]]がある。 ==概要== [[東京都]][[大田区]][[東六郷]]にある[[六郷神社|八幡塚六郷神社]]より氏子の移住と共に、大師河原干拓の総鎮守として六郷神社の[[祭神]][[応神天皇]]の御子である[[仁徳天皇]]を淀川治水工事の実績から祀ったのがはじまりとされている。創建年代は、明白でないが[[永正]]17年（[[1520年]]）から[[弘治 (日本)|弘治]]元年（[[1555年]]）にかけて実施された検地に基づいて作成された『[[小田原衆所領役帳]]』[[永禄]]2年（[[1559年]]）に朱印地三石と記されていることが初見となるのを目安とする。創建以来川崎大師[[平間寺]]に属しその鎮守社とされてきたが、[[明治]]の[[神仏分離]]の際に平間寺から独立し、平間寺38世、佐伯隆基の親族が宮司となった。 ==祭事== [[節分]]の際には、『[[豆まき]]』が行われる。いわゆる『豆まき』とは違い、[[駄菓子]]や[[煎餅]]なども蒔かれるため、子供たちで賑わう。[[1994年]]の節分祭の際には、豆撒きの福豆の中に恵方巻用の海苔の引き換え券を入れたり、社殿の前で[[年男・年女|年女・年男]]が恵方に向かって一斉に[[恵方巻]]を食べる行事を始めた。関東で[[恵方巻]]を公の行事として行った関東で唯一の神社とも言われている。 また、大師河原村は長十郎梨の発見地であるため、長十郎梨収穫祭が行われる。 ==境内社== * [[金山神社 (川崎市)|金山神社]] - 「かなまら祭」で知られる。元は[[川崎大師駅|大師駅]]前に鎮座。伊勢町の伊勢社と大師駅前の稲荷社が合祀されている。 * 藤森稲荷神社 * 大鷲神社 - 11月酉の日に「[[酉の市]]」が行われる * 厳島神社（郷土資料館内） また、境内には郷土資料館（社務所2階）と若宮幼稚園が併設されている。 ==文化財== * 若宮八幡宮神楽：境内で行われる[[囃子]][[神楽]]。2018年（平成30年）11月に[[川崎市地域文化財]]（[[無形民俗文化財]]）の決定を受けた[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html 第1回川崎市地域文化財を決定しました（川崎市教育委員会文化財課）]。 ==氏子区域== 氏子区域はおおむね以下の通り。 * 旧[[川崎町 (神奈川県)|川崎町]] ** 旧久根崎町、旧[[東京府]][[荏原郡]]雑色村 ： [[鈴木町 (川崎市)|鈴木町]] ** 旧砂子町 ： [[駅前本町 (川崎市)|駅前本町]] * 旧[[大師町|大師河原村→大師町]] ** 旧大師河原村 ：伊勢町、観音、昭和、大師河原、台町、藤崎 ** 旧川中島村 ： [[川中島 (川崎市)|川中島]]、観音、大師駅前、大師本町、大師町、東門前 ** 旧稲荷新田 ： 江川、[[小島町 (川崎市)|小島町]]、塩浜、昭和、田町、出来野、[[殿町 (川崎市)|殿町]]、日ノ出、四谷上町、四谷下町 ** 旧池上新田 ： 池上新町、池上町 * 旧[[田島町 (神奈川県)|田島村→田島町]] ** 旧大島村 ： 浅野町 ** 旧小田地先埋立地 ： 大川町、白石町 * 埋立地 ： [[浮島町 (川崎市)|浮島町]]、[[扇島]]、[[千鳥町 (川崎市)|千鳥町]]、中瀬、[[東扇島]]、水江町、夜光 == 歴代宮司 == {| class="wikitable" ! 世代 !! 宮司 |- | 初代 | 中村員彦 |- | 2代 | 中村博見 |- | 3代 | 中村博光 |- | 4代 | 中村博彦 　 |- | 5代 | 中村紀美子 |- |6代 |中村博行 |} ==兼務社== * [[水神社 (川崎市)|水神社]]（殿町） - 法人名称は宗教法人水神社 * [[厳島神社 (川崎市川崎区日ノ出)|厳島神社]]（日之出） - 法人名称は嚴島神社 * [[厳島神社 (川崎市川崎区田町)|厳島神社]]（田町） - 法人名称は嚴島神社 * [[汐留稲荷神社]]（池上新町） * [[稲荷神社 (川崎市川崎区塩浜)|稲荷神社]]（塩浜） * [[稲荷神社 (川崎市川崎区田町)|稲荷神社]]（田町） - 法人名称は宗教法人稲荷神社 * [[神明神社 (川崎市川崎区川中島)|神明神社]]（川中島） - 法人名称は宗教法人神明神社 * [[神明神社 (川崎市川崎区塩浜)|神明神社]]（塩浜） == アクセス == * [[京急大師線]] [[川崎大師駅]] 南口から徒歩3分 * [[川崎鶴見臨港バス]][[川崎鶴見臨港バス塩浜営業所#川23系統（大師線）|川23系統]] 若宮八幡前下車 ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[金山神社 (川崎市)|金山神社]] * [[若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)|若宮八幡宮]] == 外部リンク == *[https://web.archive.org/web/20051218123032/http://tomuraya.co.jp/wakamiya.htm 若宮八幡宮公式サイト] - 2020年1月27日時点で、Topページは文字化けしていますが、文字化け部分をクリックすると閲覧可能です。 *[https://x.com/kanayamajinja 若宮八幡宮🏳️‍🌈🏳️‍⚧️金山神社(かなまら様)公式] - X *[https://www.facebook.com/wakamiyasama/ 若宮八幡宮] - Facebook * [http://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/osirase/sigaku/shuhou/seido/meibo/kawasakiku.html 川崎区宗教法人名簿] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わかみやはちまんくう}} [[Category:川崎市の神社]] [[Category:川崎区の建築物]] [[Category:川崎区の歴史]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:仁徳天皇]]

{{Shrine | name=Wakamiya Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[image:Wakamiya Hachimangu 01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|4.7|N|139|43|28.7|E}}}} | location=Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki City, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan, Daishi Ekimae 2-chome 13-16 | Position={{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=32|lat_sec=4.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=43|lon_sec=28.7|label=Wakamiya Hachimangu|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | deity={{ill|Nintoku-tennō|ja|仁徳天皇}} | Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrines under the modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度#諸社}} | Foundation=Around {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 2 (1559) | festival= }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangu''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Daishi Ekimae|ja|大師駅前}}, {{ill|Kawasaki-ku|ja|川崎区}}, {{ill|Kawasaki City|ja|川崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It served as the guardian shrine for the former {{ill|Daishikawara|ja|大師河原}} area. Its precincts include the {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}}, which is famous for the {{ill|Kanamara Matsuri|ja|かなまら祭}}. ==Overview== The shrine's origins trace back to the transfer of the {{ill|Rokugō Shrine|ja|六郷神社}} deity in {{ill|Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|ja|大田区東六郷}}, Tokyo, by local residents moving to {{ill|Daishikawara reclaimed land|ja|大師河原干拓}}. To honor the achievements of Emperor Nintoku in river management of the Yodo River, they enshrined him as a guardian deity. Although the exact date of the shrine’s foundation is unclear, records such as the land survey-based {{ill|Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|ja|小田原衆所領役帳}} from Eiroku 2 (1559) first mention the shrine receiving three koku of red-seal land. Initially subordinate to the {{ill|Heikenji|ja|平間寺}} (Kawasaki Daishi), the shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} policy of the Meiji era, with a priest from Heikenji’s 38th head, Saeki Takamoto, serving as its chief priest. ==Festivals== During {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}, the shrine hosts a {{ill|Setsubun|ja|豆まき|lt=Bean-Throwing Festival}}, featuring not only beans but also sweets and rice crackers, attracting many children. In 1994, the shrine initiated an event during the Setsubun Festival where fortune beans included tickets for seaweed used in {{ill|Ehomaki|ja|恵方巻}}, and {{ill|Toshiotoko|ja|年男}} and {{ill|Toshionna|ja|年女}} participants ate Ehomaki facing the year's lucky direction. This was reportedly the first public Ehomaki event in the Kanto region. Additionally, as Daishikawara village was the discovery site of the {{ill|Nagajūrō Pear|ja|長十郎梨}}, the shrine holds a Nagajūrō Pear Harvest Festival. ==Sub-Shrines== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} - Known for the Kanamara Matsuri. Originally located near Daishi Station. Includes the {{ill|Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|伊勢社}} and an Inari shrine. * {{ill|Fujimori Inari Shrine|ja|藤森稲荷神社}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社}} - Hosts the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival in November. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} - Located within the Local History Museum. The precinct also houses the Local History Museum (on the second floor of the shrine office) and Wakamiya Kindergarten. ==Cultural Properties== * '''Wakamiya Hachimangu Kagura:''' A type of {{ill|Hayashi Kagura|ja|囃子神楽}} performed on the grounds. Designated as a {{ill|Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|ja|川崎市地域文化財}} in November 2018.[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Decision on Kawasaki City’s First Regional Cultural Properties (Kawasaki City Board of Education)] ==Parish Districts== The shrine’s parish covers the following areas: * Former {{ill|Kawasaki Town|ja|川崎町}}: * Former Kuranezaki and Zōshiki Villages: {{ill|Suzuki Town|ja|鈴木町}} * Former Sunako Town: {{ill|Ekimoto Town|ja|駅前本町}} * Former {{ill|Daishi Town|ja|大師町}}: * Former Daishikawara Village: Ise Town, Kannon, Shōwa, Daishikawara, Dai Town, Fujisaki * Former Kawanakashima Village: Kawanakashima, Kannon, Daishi Ekimae, Daishi Honmachi, Daishi Town, Higashimonzen * Former Inarishinden: Egawa, {{ill|Kojima Town|ja|小島町}}, Shiohama, Shōwa, Tamachi, Dekino, {{ill|Tonomachi|ja|殿町}}, Hinode, Yotsuya Kami-machi, Yotsuya Shimo-machi * Former Ikegami Shinden: Ikegami Shin-machi, Ikegami Town * Former {{ill|Tajima Town|ja|田島町}}: * Former Ōshima Village: Asano Town * Former Kodachi Landfill: Ōkawa Town, Shiroishi Town * Reclaimed Land: {{ill|Ukishima Town|ja|浮島町}}, {{ill|Ogishima|ja|扇島}}, {{ill|Chidori Town|ja|千鳥町}}, Nakase, {{ill|Higashi Ogishima|ja|東扇島}}, Mizue Town, Yakō ==Lineage of Chief Priests== {| class="wikitable" ! Generation !! Chief Priest |- | First || Nakamura Kazuhiko |- | Second || Nakamura Hiroken |- | Third || Nakamura Hiroko |- | Fourth || Nakamura Hirohiko |- | Fifth || Nakamura Kimiko |- | Sixth || Nakamura Hiroyuki |} ==Associated Shrines== * {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|厳島神社}} (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * {{ill|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)|ja|稲荷神社}} (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * {{ill|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|神明神社}} (Kawanakashima and Shiohama). ==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi Station|ja|川崎大師駅}} on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス}} Kawasai 23 line. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==See Also== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachimangu (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮}} ==External Links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20051218123032/http://tomuraya.co.jp/wakamiya.htm Wakamiya Hachimangu Official Site] - Archived on January 27, 2020. * [https://www.facebook.com/wakamiyasama/ Wakamiya Hachimangu] - Facebook * [http://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/osirase/sigaku/shuhou/seido/meibo/kawasakiku.html Kawasaki City Religious Corporation Registry] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Guardian Shrines]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B7%9D%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]]

{{Shrine | name=Wakamiya Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[image:Wakamiya Hachimangu 01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|4.7|N|139|43|28.7|E}}}} | location=Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki City, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan, Daishi Ekimae 2-chome 13-16 | Position={{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=32|lat_sec=4.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=43|lon_sec=28.7|label=Wakamiya Hachimangu|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | deity={{ill|Nintoku-tennō|ja|仁徳天皇}} | Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrines under the modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度#諸社}} | Foundation=Around {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 2 (1559) | festival= }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangu''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Daishi Ekimae|ja|大師駅前}}, {{ill|Kawasaki-ku|ja|川崎区}}, {{ill|Kawasaki City|ja|川崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It served as the guardian shrine for the former {{ill|Daishikawara|ja|大師河原}} area. Its precincts include the {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}}, which is famous for the {{ill|Kanamara Matsuri|ja|かなまら祭}}. ==Overview== The shrine's origins trace back to the transfer of the {{ill|Rokugō Shrine|ja|六郷神社}} deity in {{ill|Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|ja|大田区東六郷}}, Tokyo, by local residents moving to {{ill|Daishikawara reclaimed land|ja|大師河原干拓}}. To honor the achievements of Emperor Nintoku in river management of the Yodo River, they enshrined him as a guardian deity. Although the exact date of the shrine’s foundation is unclear, records such as the land survey-based {{ill|Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|ja|小田原衆所領役帳}} from Eiroku 2 (1559) first mention the shrine receiving three koku of red-seal land. Initially subordinate to the {{ill|Heikenji|ja|平間寺}} (Kawasaki Daishi), the shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} policy of the Meiji era, with a priest from Heikenji’s 38th head, Saeki Takamoto, serving as its chief priest. ==Festivals== During {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}, the shrine hosts a {{ill|Setsubun|ja|豆まき|lt=Bean-Throwing Festival}}, featuring not only beans but also sweets and rice crackers, attracting many children. In 1994, the shrine initiated an event during the Setsubun Festival where fortune beans included tickets for seaweed used in {{ill|Ehomaki|ja|恵方巻}}, and {{ill|Toshiotoko|ja|年男}} and {{ill|Toshionna|ja|年女}} participants ate Ehomaki facing the year's lucky direction. This was reportedly the first public Ehomaki event in the Kanto region. Additionally, as Daishikawara village was the discovery site of the {{ill|Nagajūrō Pear|ja|長十郎梨}}, the shrine holds a Nagajūrō Pear Harvest Festival. ==Sub-Shrines== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} - Known for the Kanamara Matsuri. Originally located near Daishi Station. Includes the {{ill|Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|伊勢社}} and an Inari shrine. * {{ill|Fujimori Inari Shrine|ja|藤森稲荷神社}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社}} - Hosts the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival in November. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} - Located within the Local History Museum. The precinct also houses the Local History Museum (on the second floor of the shrine office) and Wakamiya Kindergarten. ==Cultural Properties== * '''Wakamiya Hachimangu Kagura:''' A type of {{ill|Hayashi Kagura|ja|囃子神楽}} performed on the grounds. Designated as a {{ill|Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|ja|川崎市地域文化財}} in November 2018.[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Decision on Kawasaki City’s First Regional Cultural Properties (Kawasaki City Board of Education)] ==Parish Districts== The shrine’s parish covers the following areas: * Former {{ill|Kawasaki Town|ja|川崎町}}: * Former Kuranezaki and Zōshiki Villages: {{ill|Suzuki Town|ja|鈴木町}} * Former Sunako Town: {{ill|Ekimoto Town|ja|駅前本町}} * Former {{ill|Daishi Town|ja|大師町}}: * Former Daishikawara Village: Ise Town, Kannon, Shōwa, Daishikawara, Dai Town, Fujisaki * Former Kawanakashima Village: Kawanakashima, Kannon, Daishi Ekimae, Daishi Honmachi, Daishi Town, Higashimonzen * Former Inarishinden: Egawa, {{ill|Kojima Town|ja|小島町}}, Shiohama, Shōwa, Tamachi, Dekino, {{ill|Tonomachi|ja|殿町}}, Hinode, Yotsuya Kami-machi, Yotsuya Shimo-machi * Former Ikegami Shinden: Ikegami Shin-machi, Ikegami Town * Former {{ill|Tajima Town|ja|田島町}}: * Former Ōshima Village: Asano Town * Former Kodachi Landfill: Ōkawa Town, Shiroishi Town * Reclaimed Land: {{ill|Ukishima Town|ja|浮島町}}, {{ill|Ogishima|ja|扇島}}, {{ill|Chidori Town|ja|千鳥町}}, Nakase, {{ill|Higashi Ogishima|ja|東扇島}}, Mizue Town, Yakō ==Lineage of Chief Priests== {| class="wikitable" ! Generation !! Chief Priest |- | First || Nakamura Kazuhiko |- | Second || Nakamura Hiroken |- | Third || Nakamura Hiroko |- | Fourth || Nakamura Hirohiko |- | Fifth || Nakamura Kimiko |- | Sixth || Nakamura Hiroyuki |} ==Associated Shrines== * {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|厳島神社}} (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * {{ill|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)|ja|稲荷神社}} (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * {{ill|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|神明神社}} (Kawanakashima and Shiohama). ==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi Station|ja|川崎大師駅}} on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス}} Kawasai 23 line. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==See Also== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachimangu (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮}} ==External Links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20051218123032/http://tomuraya.co.jp/wakamiya.htm Wakamiya Hachimangu Official Site] - Archived on January 27, 2020. * [https://www.facebook.com/wakamiyasama/ Wakamiya Hachimangu] - Facebook * [http://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/osirase/sigaku/shuhou/seido/meibo/kawasakiku.html Kawasaki City Religious Corporation Registry] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B7%9D%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|4.7|N|139|43|28.7|E}}}} | location=Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki City, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan, Daishi Ekimae 2-chome 13-16 | Position={{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=32|lat_sec=4.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=43|lon_sec=28.7|label=Wakamiya Hachimangu|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | deity={{ill|Nintoku-tennō|ja|仁徳天皇}} | Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrines under the modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度#諸社}} | Foundation=Around {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 2 (1559) | festival= }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangu''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Daishi Ekimae|ja|大師駅前}}, {{ill|Kawasaki-ku|ja|川崎区}}, {{ill|Kawasaki City|ja|川崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. {{Shrine | name=Wakamiya Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[image:Wakamiya Hachimangu 01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|4.7|N|139|43|28.7|E}}}} | location=Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki City, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan, Daishi Ekimae 2-chome 13-16 | Position={{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=32|lat_sec=4.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=43|lon_sec=28.7|label=Wakamiya Hachimangu|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | deity={{ill|Nintoku-tennō|ja|仁徳天皇}} | Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrines under the modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度#諸社}} | Foundation=Around {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 2 (1559) | festival= }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangu''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Daishi Ekimae|ja|大師駅前}}, {{ill|Kawasaki-ku|ja|川崎区}}, {{ill|Kawasaki City|ja|川崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It served as the guardian shrine for the former {{ill|Daishikawara|ja|大師河原}} area. Its precincts include the {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}}, which is famous for the {{ill|Kanamara Matsuri|ja|かなまら祭}}. ==Overview== The shrine's origins trace back to the transfer of the {{ill|Rokugō Shrine|ja|六郷神社}} deity in {{ill|Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|ja|大田区東六郷}}, Tokyo, by local residents moving to {{ill|Daishikawara reclaimed land|ja|大師河原干拓}}. To honor the achievements of Emperor Nintoku in river management of the Yodo River, they enshrined him as a guardian deity. Although the exact date of the shrine’s foundation is unclear, records such as the land survey-based {{ill|Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|ja|小田原衆所領役帳}} from Eiroku 2 (1559) first mention the shrine receiving three koku of red-seal land. Initially subordinate to the {{ill|Heikenji|ja|平間寺}} (Kawasaki Daishi), the shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} policy of the Meiji era, with a priest from Heikenji’s 38th head, Saeki Takamoto, serving as its chief priest. ==Festivals== During {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}, the shrine hosts a {{ill|Setsubun|ja|豆まき|lt=Bean-Throwing Festival}}, featuring not only beans but also sweets and rice crackers, attracting many children. In 1994, the shrine initiated an event during the Setsubun Festival where fortune beans included tickets for seaweed used in {{ill|Ehomaki|ja|恵方巻}}, and {{ill|Toshiotoko|ja|年男}} and {{ill|Toshionna|ja|年女}} participants ate Ehomaki facing the year's lucky direction. This was reportedly the first public Ehomaki event in the Kanto region. Additionally, as Daishikawara village was the discovery site of the {{ill|Nagajūrō Pear|ja|長十郎梨}}, the shrine holds a Nagajūrō Pear Harvest Festival. ==Sub-Shrines== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} - Known for the Kanamara Matsuri. Originally located near Daishi Station. Includes the {{ill|Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|伊勢社}} and an Inari shrine. * {{ill|Fujimori Inari Shrine|ja|藤森稲荷神社}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社}} - Hosts the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival in November. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} - Located within the Local History Museum. The precinct also houses the Local History Museum (on the second floor of the shrine office) and Wakamiya Kindergarten. ==Cultural Properties== * '''Wakamiya Hachimangu Kagura:''' A type of {{ill|Hayashi Kagura|ja|囃子神楽}} performed on the grounds. Designated as a {{ill|Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|ja|川崎市地域文化財}} in November 2018.[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Decision on Kawasaki City’s First Regional Cultural Properties (Kawasaki City Board of Education)] ==Parish Districts== The shrine’s parish covers the following areas: * Former {{ill|Kawasaki Town|ja|川崎町}}: * Former Kuranezaki and Zōshiki Villages: {{ill|Suzuki Town|ja|鈴木町}} * Former Sunako Town: {{ill|Ekimoto Town|ja|駅前本町}} * Former {{ill|Daishi Town|ja|大師町}}: * Former Daishikawara Village: Ise Town, Kannon, Shōwa, Daishikawara, Dai Town, Fujisaki * Former Kawanakashima Village: Kawanakashima, Kannon, Daishi Ekimae, Daishi Honmachi, Daishi Town, Higashimonzen * Former Inarishinden: Egawa, {{ill|Kojima Town|ja|小島町}}, Shiohama, Shōwa, Tamachi, Dekino, {{ill|Tonomachi|ja|殿町}}, Hinode, Yotsuya Kami-machi, Yotsuya Shimo-machi * Former Ikegami Shinden: Ikegami Shin-machi, Ikegami Town * Former {{ill|Tajima Town|ja|田島町}}: * Former Ōshima Village: Asano Town * Former Kodachi Landfill: Ōkawa Town, Shiroishi Town * Reclaimed Land: {{ill|Ukishima Town|ja|浮島町}}, {{ill|Ogishima|ja|扇島}}, {{ill|Chidori Town|ja|千鳥町}}, Nakase, {{ill|Higashi Ogishima|ja|東扇島}}, Mizue Town, Yakō ==Lineage of Chief Priests== {| class="wikitable" ! Generation !! Chief Priest |- | First || Nakamura Kazuhiko |- | Second || Nakamura Hiroken |- | Third || Nakamura Hiroko |- | Fourth || Nakamura Hirohiko |- | Fifth || Nakamura Kimiko |- | Sixth || Nakamura Hiroyuki |} ==Associated Shrines== * {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|厳島神社}} (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * {{ill|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)|ja|稲荷神社}} (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * {{ill|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|神明神社}} (Kawanakashima and Shiohama). ==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi Station|ja|川崎大師駅}} on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス}} Kawasai 23 line. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==See Also== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachimangu (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮}} ==External Links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20051218123032/http://tomuraya.co.jp/wakamiya.htm Wakamiya Hachimangu Official Site] - Archived on January 27, 2020. * [https://www.facebook.com/wakamiyasama/ Wakamiya Hachimangu] - Facebook * [http://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/osirase/sigaku/shuhou/seido/meibo/kawasakiku.html Kawasaki City Religious Corporation Registry] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B7%9D%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine | name=Wakamiya Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[image:Wakamiya Hachimangu 01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden

Archived on January 27, 2020. * [https://www.facebook.com/wakamiyasama/ Wakamiya Hachimangu] - Facebook * [http://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/osirase/sigaku/shuhou/seido/meibo/kawasakiku.html Kawasaki City Religious Corporation Registry] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B7%9D%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine | name=Wakamiya Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[image:Wakamiya Hachimangu 01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|4.7|N|139|43|28.7|E}}}} | location=Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki City, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan, Daishi Ekimae 2-chome 13-16 | Position={{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=32|lat_sec=4.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=43|lon_sec=28.7|label=Wakamiya Hachimangu|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | deity={{ill|Nintoku-tennō|ja|仁徳天皇}} | Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrines under the modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度#諸社}} | Foundation=Around {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 2 (1559) | festival= }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangu''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Daishi Ekimae|ja|大師駅前}}, {{ill|Kawasaki-ku|ja|川崎区}}, {{ill|Kawasaki City|ja|川崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It served as the guardian shrine for the former {{ill|Daishikawara|ja|大師河原}} area. Its precincts include the {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}}, which is famous for the {{ill|Kanamara Matsuri|ja|かなまら祭}}. ==Overview== The shrine's origins trace back to the transfer of the {{ill|Rokugō Shrine|ja|六郷神社}} deity in {{ill|Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|ja|大田区東六郷}}, Tokyo, by local residents moving to {{ill|Daishikawara reclaimed land|ja|大師河原干拓}}. To honor the achievements of Emperor Nintoku in river management of the Yodo River, they enshrined him as a guardian deity. Although the exact date of the shrine’s foundation is unclear, records such as the land survey-based {{ill|Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|ja|小田原衆所領役帳}} from Eiroku 2 (1559) first mention the shrine receiving three koku of red-seal land. Initially subordinate to the {{ill|Heikenji|ja|平間寺}} (Kawasaki Daishi), the shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} policy of the Meiji era, with a priest from Heikenji’s 38th head, Saeki Takamoto, serving as its chief priest. ==Festivals== During {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}, the shrine hosts a {{ill|Setsubun|ja|豆まき|lt=Bean-Throwing Festival}}, featuring not only beans but also sweets and rice crackers, attracting many children. In 1994, the shrine initiated an event during the Setsubun Festival where fortune beans included tickets for seaweed used in {{ill|Ehomaki|ja|恵方巻}}, and {{ill|Toshiotoko|ja|年男}} and {{ill|Toshionna|ja|年女}} participants ate Ehomaki facing the year's lucky direction. This was reportedly the first public Ehomaki event in the Kanto region. Additionally, as Daishikawara village was the discovery site of the {{ill|Nagajūrō Pear|ja|長十郎梨}}, the shrine holds a Nagajūrō Pear Harvest Festival. ==Sub-Shrines== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} - Known for the Kanamara Matsuri. Originally located near Daishi Station. Includes the {{ill|Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|伊勢社}} and an Inari shrine. * {{ill|Fujimori Inari Shrine|ja|藤森稲荷神社}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社}} - Hosts the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival in November. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} - Located within the Local History Museum. The precinct also houses the Local History Museum (on the second floor of the shrine office) and Wakamiya Kindergarten. ==Cultural Properties== * '''Wakamiya Hachimangu Kagura:''' A type of {{ill|Hayashi Kagura|ja|囃子神楽}} performed on the grounds. Designated as a {{ill|Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|ja|川崎市地域文化財}} in November 2018.[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Decision on Kawasaki City’s First Regional Cultural Properties (Kawasaki City Board of Education)] ==Parish Districts== The shrine’s parish covers the following areas: * Former {{ill|Kawasaki Town|ja|川崎町}}: * Former Kuranezaki and Zōshiki Villages: {{ill|Suzuki Town|ja|鈴木町}} * Former Sunako Town: {{ill|Ekimoto Town|ja|駅前本町}} * Former {{ill|Daishi Town|ja|大師町}}: * Former Daishikawara Village: Ise Town, Kannon, Shōwa, Daishikawara, Dai Town, Fujisaki * Former Kawanakashima Village: Kawanakashima, Kannon, Daishi Ekimae, Daishi Honmachi, Daishi Town, Higashimonzen * Former Inarishinden: Egawa, {{ill|Kojima Town|ja|小島町}}, Shiohama, Shōwa, Tamachi, Dekino, {{ill|Tonomachi|ja|殿町}}, Hinode, Yotsuya Kami-machi, Yotsuya Shimo-machi * Former Ikegami Shinden: Ikegami Shin-machi, Ikegami Town * Former {{ill|Tajima Town|ja|田島町}}: * Former Ōshima Village: Asano Town * Former Kodachi Landfill: Ōkawa Town, Shiroishi Town * Reclaimed Land: {{ill|Ukishima Town|ja|浮島町}}, {{ill|Ogishima|ja|扇島}}, {{ill|Chidori Town|ja|千鳥町}}, Nakase, {{ill|Higashi Ogishima|ja|東扇島}}, Mizue Town, Yakō ==Lineage of Chief Priests== {| class="wikitable" ! Generation !! Chief Priest |- | First || Nakamura Kazuhiko |- | Second || Nakamura Hiroken |- | Third || Nakamura Hiroko |- | Fourth || Nakamura Hirohiko |- | Fifth || Nakamura Kimiko |- | Sixth || Nakamura Hiroyuki |} ==Associated Shrines== * {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|厳島神社}} (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * {{ill|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)|ja|稲荷神社}} (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * {{ill|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|神明神社}} (Kawanakashima and Shiohama). ==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi Station|ja|川崎大師駅}} on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス}} Kawasai 23 line. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==See Also== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachimangu (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮}} ==External Links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20051218123032/http://tomuraya.co.jp/wakamiya.htm Wakamiya Hachimangu Official Site] - Archived on January 27, 2020. * [https://www.facebook.com/wakamiyasama/ Wakamiya Hachimangu] - Facebook * [http://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/osirase/sigaku/shuhou/seido/meibo/kawasakiku.html Kawasaki City Religious Corporation Registry] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B7%9D%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]]

Designated as a {{ill|Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|ja|川崎市地域文化財}} in November 2018.[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Decision on Kawasaki City’s First Regional Cultural Properties (Kawasaki City Board of Education)] ==Parish Districts== The shrine’s parish covers the following areas: * Former {{ill|Kawasaki Town|ja|川崎町}}: * Former Kuranezaki and Zōshiki Villages: {{ill|Suzuki Town|ja|鈴木町}} * Former Sunako Town: {{ill|Ekimoto Town|ja|駅前本町}} * Former {{ill|Daishi Town|ja|大師町}}: * Former Daishikawara Village: Ise Town, Kannon, Shōwa, Daishikawara, Dai Town, Fujisaki * Former Kawanakashima Village: Kawanakashima, Kannon, Daishi Ekimae, Daishi Honmachi, Daishi Town, Higashimonzen * Former Inarishinden: Egawa, {{ill|Kojima Town|ja|小島町}}, Shiohama, Shōwa, Tamachi, Dekino, {{ill|Tonomachi|ja|殿町}}, Hinode, Yotsuya Kami-machi, Yotsuya Shimo-machi * Former Ikegami Shinden: Ikegami Shin-machi, Ikegami Town * Former {{ill|Tajima Town|ja|田島町}}: * Former Ōshima Village: Asano Town * Former Kodachi Landfill: Ōkawa Town, Shiroishi Town * Reclaimed Land: {{ill|Ukishima Town|ja|浮島町}}, {{ill|Ogishima|ja|扇島}}, {{ill|Chidori Town|ja|千鳥町}}, Nakase, {{ill|Higashi Ogishima|ja|東扇島}}, Mizue Town, Yakō ==Lineage of Chief Priests== {| class="wikitable" ! Generation !! Chief Priest |- | First || Nakamura Kazuhiko |- | Second || Nakamura Hiroken |- | Third || Nakamura Hiroko |- | Fourth || Nakamura Hirohiko |- | Fifth || Nakamura Kimiko |- | Sixth || Nakamura Hiroyuki |} ==Associated Shrines== * {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|厳島神社}} (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * {{ill|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)|ja|稲荷神社}} (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * {{ill|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|神明神社}} (Kawanakashima and Shiohama). ==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi Station|ja|川崎大師駅}} on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス}} Kawasai 23 line. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==See Also== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachimangu (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮}} ==External Links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20051218123032/http://tomuraya.co.jp/wakamiya.htm Wakamiya Hachimangu Official Site] - Archived on January 27, 2020. * [https://www.facebook.com/wakamiyasama/ Wakamiya Hachimangu] - Facebook * [http://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/osirase/sigaku/shuhou/seido/meibo/kawasakiku.html Kawasaki City Religious Corporation Registry] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B7%9D%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:11]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name=Wakamiya Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[image:Wakamiya Hachimangu 01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|4.7|N|139|43|28.7|E}}}} | location=Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki City, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan, Daishi Ekimae 2-chome 13-16 | Position={{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=32|lat_sec=4.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=43|lon_sec=28.7|label=Wakamiya Hachimangu|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | deity={{ill|Nintoku-tennō|ja|仁徳天皇}} | Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrines under the modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度#諸社}} | Foundation=Around {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 2 (1559) | festival= }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangu''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Daishi Ekimae|ja|大師駅前}}, {{ill|Kawasaki-ku|ja|川崎区}}, {{ill|Kawasaki City|ja|川崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It served as the guardian shrine for the former {{ill|Daishikawara|ja|大師河原}} area. Its precincts include the {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}}, which is famous for the {{ill|Kanamara Matsuri|ja|かなまら祭}}. ==Overview== The shrine's origins trace back to the transfer of the {{ill|Rokugō Shrine|ja|六郷神社}} deity in {{ill|Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|ja|大田区東六郷}}, Tokyo, by local residents moving to {{ill|Daishikawara reclaimed land|ja|大師河原干拓}}. To honor the achievements of Emperor Nintoku in river management of the Yodo River, they enshrined him as a guardian deity. Although the exact date of the shrine’s foundation is unclear, records such as the land survey-based {{ill|Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|ja|小田原衆所領役帳}} from Eiroku 2 (1559) first mention the shrine receiving three koku of red-seal land. Initially subordinate to the {{ill|Heikenji|ja|平間寺}} (Kawasaki Daishi), the shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} policy of the Meiji era, with a priest from Heikenji’s 38th head, Saeki Takamoto, serving as its chief priest. ==Festivals== During {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}, the shrine hosts a {{ill|Setsubun|ja|豆まき|lt=Bean-Throwing Festival}}, featuring not only beans but also sweets and rice crackers, attracting many children. In 1994, the shrine initiated an event during the Setsubun Festival where fortune beans included tickets for seaweed used in {{ill|Ehomaki|ja|恵方巻}}, and {{ill|Toshiotoko|ja|年男}} and {{ill|Toshionna|ja|年女}} participants ate Ehomaki facing the year's lucky direction. This was reportedly the first public Ehomaki event in the Kanto region. Additionally, as Daishikawara village was the discovery site of the {{ill|Nagajūrō Pear|ja|長十郎梨}}, the shrine holds a Nagajūrō Pear Harvest Festival. ==Sub-Shrines== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} - Known for the Kanamara Matsuri. Originally located near Daishi Station. Includes the {{ill|Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|伊勢社}} and an Inari shrine. * {{ill|Fujimori Inari Shrine|ja|藤森稲荷神社}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社}} - Hosts the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival in November. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} - Located within the Local History Museum. The precinct also houses the Local History Museum (on the second floor of the shrine office) and Wakamiya Kindergarten. ==Cultural Properties== * '''Wakamiya Hachimangu Kagura:''' A type of {{ill|Hayashi Kagura|ja|囃子神楽}} performed on the grounds. Designated as a {{ill|Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|ja|川崎市地域文化財}} in November 2018.[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Decision on Kawasaki City’s First Regional Cultural Properties (Kawasaki City Board of Education)] ==Parish Districts== The shrine’s parish covers the following areas: * Former {{ill|Kawasaki Town|ja|川崎町}}: * Former Kuranezaki and Zōshiki Villages: {{ill|Suzuki Town|ja|鈴木町}} * Former Sunako Town: {{ill|Ekimoto Town|ja|駅前本町}} * Former {{ill|Daishi Town|ja|大師町}}: * Former Daishikawara Village: Ise Town, Kannon, Shōwa, Daishikawara, Dai Town, Fujisaki * Former Kawanakashima Village: Kawanakashima, Kannon, Daishi Ekimae, Daishi Honmachi, Daishi Town, Higashimonzen * Former Inarishinden: Egawa, {{ill|Kojima Town|ja|小島町}}, Shiohama, Shōwa, Tamachi, Dekino, {{ill|Tonomachi|ja|殿町}}, Hinode, Yotsuya Kami-machi, Yotsuya Shimo-machi * Former Ikegami Shinden: Ikegami Shin-machi, Ikegami Town * Former {{ill|Tajima Town|ja|田島町}}: * Former Ōshima Village: Asano Town * Former Kodachi Landfill: Ōkawa Town, Shiroishi Town * Reclaimed Land: {{ill|Ukishima Town|ja|浮島町}}, {{ill|Ogishima|ja|扇島}}, {{ill|Chidori Town|ja|千鳥町}}, Nakase, {{ill|Higashi Ogishima|ja|東扇島}}, Mizue Town, Yakō ==Lineage of Chief Priests== {| class="wikitable" ! Generation !! Chief Priest |- | First || Nakamura Kazuhiko |- | Second || Nakamura Hiroken |- | Third || Nakamura Hiroko |- | Fourth || Nakamura Hirohiko |- | Fifth || Nakamura Kimiko |- | Sixth || Nakamura Hiroyuki |} ==Associated Shrines== * {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|厳島神社}} (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * {{ill|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)|ja|稲荷神社}} (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * {{ill|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|神明神社}} (Kawanakashima and Shiohama). ==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi Station|ja|川崎大師駅}} on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス}} Kawasai 23 line. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==See Also== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachimangu (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮}} ==External Links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20051218123032/http://tomuraya.co.jp/wakamiya.htm Wakamiya Hachimangu Official Site] - Archived on January 27, 2020. * [https://www.facebook.com/wakamiyasama/ Wakamiya Hachimangu] - Facebook * [http://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/osirase/sigaku/shuhou/seido/meibo/kawasakiku.html Kawasaki City Religious Corporation Registry] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B7%9D%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]]

{{Shrine | name=Wakamiya Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[image:Wakamiya Hachimangu 01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|4.7|N|139|43|28.7|E}}}} | location=Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki City, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan, Daishi Ekimae 2-chome 13-16 | Position={{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=32|lat_sec=4.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=43|lon_sec=28.7|label=Wakamiya Hachimangu|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | deity={{ill|Nintoku-tennō|ja|仁徳天皇}} | Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrines under the modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度#諸社}} | Foundation=Around {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 2 (1559) | festival= }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangu''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Daishi Ekimae|ja|大師駅前}}, {{ill|Kawasaki-ku|ja|川崎区}}, {{ill|Kawasaki City|ja|川崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It served as the guardian shrine for the former {{ill|Daishikawara|ja|大師河原}} area. Its precincts include the {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}}, which is famous for the {{ill|Kanamara Matsuri|ja|かなまら祭}}. ==Overview== The shrine's origins trace back to the transfer of the {{ill|Rokugō Shrine|ja|六郷神社}} deity in {{ill|Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|ja|大田区東六郷}}, Tokyo, by local residents moving to {{ill|Daishikawara reclaimed land|ja|大師河原干拓}}. To honor the achievements of Emperor Nintoku in river management of the Yodo River, they enshrined him as a guardian deity. Although the exact date of the shrine’s foundation is unclear, records such as the land survey-based {{ill|Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|ja|小田原衆所領役帳}} from Eiroku 2 (1559) first mention the shrine receiving three koku of red-seal land. Initially subordinate to the {{ill|Heikenji|ja|平間寺}} (Kawasaki Daishi), the shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} policy of the Meiji era, with a priest from Heikenji’s 38th head, Saeki Takamoto, serving as its chief priest. ==Festivals== During {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}, the shrine hosts a {{ill|Setsubun|ja|豆まき|lt=Bean-Throwing Festival}}, featuring not only beans but also sweets and rice crackers, attracting many children. In 1994, the shrine initiated an event during the Setsubun Festival where fortune beans included tickets for seaweed used in {{ill|Ehomaki|ja|恵方巻}}, and {{ill|Toshiotoko|ja|年男}} and {{ill|Toshionna|ja|年女}} participants ate Ehomaki facing the year's lucky direction. This was reportedly the first public Ehomaki event in the Kanto region. Additionally, as Daishikawara village was the discovery site of the {{ill|Nagajūrō Pear|ja|長十郎梨}}, the shrine holds a Nagajūrō Pear Harvest Festival. ==Sub-Shrines== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} - Known for the Kanamara Matsuri. Originally located near Daishi Station. Includes the {{ill|Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|伊勢社}} and an Inari shrine. * {{ill|Fujimori Inari Shrine|ja|藤森稲荷神社}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社}} - Hosts the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival in November. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} - Located within the Local History Museum. The precinct also houses the Local History Museum (on the second floor of the shrine office) and Wakamiya Kindergarten. ==Cultural Properties== * '''Wakamiya Hachimangu Kagura:''' A type of {{ill|Hayashi Kagura|ja|囃子神楽}} performed on the grounds. Designated as a {{ill|Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|ja|川崎市地域文化財}} in November 2018.[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Decision on Kawasaki City’s First Regional Cultural Properties (Kawasaki City Board of Education)] ==Parish Districts== The shrine’s parish covers the following areas: * Former {{ill|Kawasaki Town|ja|川崎町}}: * Former Kuranezaki and Zōshiki Villages: {{ill|Suzuki Town|ja|鈴木町}} * Former Sunako Town: {{ill|Ekimoto Town|ja|駅前本町}} * Former {{ill|Daishi Town|ja|大師町}}: * Former Daishikawara Village: Ise Town, Kannon, Shōwa, Daishikawara, Dai Town, Fujisaki * Former Kawanakashima Village: Kawanakashima, Kannon, Daishi Ekimae, Daishi Honmachi, Daishi Town, Higashimonzen * Former Inarishinden: Egawa, {{ill|Kojima Town|ja|小島町}}, Shiohama, Shōwa, Tamachi, Dekino, {{ill|Tonomachi|ja|殿町}}, Hinode, Yotsuya Kami-machi, Yotsuya Shimo-machi * Former Ikegami Shinden: Ikegami Shin-machi, Ikegami Town * Former {{ill|Tajima Town|ja|田島町}}: * Former Ōshima Village: Asano Town * Former Kodachi Landfill: Ōkawa Town, Shiroishi Town * Reclaimed Land: {{ill|Ukishima Town|ja|浮島町}}, {{ill|Ogishima|ja|扇島}}, {{ill|Chidori Town|ja|千鳥町}}, Nakase, {{ill|Higashi Ogishima|ja|東扇島}}, Mizue Town, Yakō ==Lineage of Chief Priests== {| class="wikitable" ! Generation !! Chief Priest |- | First || Nakamura Kazuhiko |- | Second || Nakamura Hiroken |- | Third || Nakamura Hiroko |- | Fourth || Nakamura Hirohiko |- | Fifth || Nakamura Kimiko |- | Sixth || Nakamura Hiroyuki |} ==Associated Shrines== * {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|厳島神社}} (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * {{ill|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)|ja|稲荷神社}} (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * {{ill|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|神明神社}} (Kawanakashima and Shiohama). ==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi Station|ja|川崎大師駅}} on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス}} Kawasai 23 line. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==See Also== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachimangu (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮}} ==External Links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20051218123032/http://tomuraya.co.jp/wakamiya.htm Wakamiya Hachimangu Official Site] - Archived on January 27, 2020. * [https://www.facebook.com/wakamiyasama/ Wakamiya Hachimangu] - Facebook * [http://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/osirase/sigaku/shuhou/seido/meibo/kawasakiku.html Kawasaki City Religious Corporation Registry] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B7%9D%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]]

Generation !! Chief Priest |- | First || Nakamura Kazuhiko |- | Second || Nakamura Hiroken |- | Third || Nakamura Hiroko |- | Fourth || Nakamura Hirohiko |- | Fifth || Nakamura Kimiko |- | Sixth || Nakamura Hiroyuki |} ==Associated Shrines== * {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|厳島神社}} (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * {{ill|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)|ja|稲荷神社}} (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * {{ill|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|神明神社}} (Kawanakashima and Shiohama). ==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi Station|ja|川崎大師駅}} on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス}} Kawasai 23 line. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==See Also== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachimangu (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮}} ==External Links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20051218123032/http://tomuraya.co.jp/wakamiya.htm Wakamiya Hachimangu Official Site] - Archived on January 27, 2020. * [https://www.facebook.com/wakamiyasama/ Wakamiya Hachimangu] - Facebook * [http://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/osirase/sigaku/shuhou/seido/meibo/kawasakiku.html Kawasaki City Religious Corporation Registry] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B7%9D%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

{{Shrine | name=Wakamiya Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[image:Wakamiya Hachimangu 01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|4.7|N|139|43|28.7|E}}}} | location=Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki City, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan, Daishi Ekimae 2-chome 13-16 | Position={{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=32|lat_sec=4.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=43|lon_sec=28.7|label=Wakamiya Hachimangu|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | deity={{ill|Nintoku-tennō|ja|仁徳天皇}} | Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrines under the modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度#諸社}} | Foundation=Around {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 2 (1559) | festival= }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangu''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Daishi Ekimae|ja|大師駅前}}, {{ill|Kawasaki-ku|ja|川崎区}}, {{ill|Kawasaki City|ja|川崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It served as the guardian shrine for the former {{ill|Daishikawara|ja|大師河原}} area. Its precincts include the {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}}, which is famous for the {{ill|Kanamara Matsuri|ja|かなまら祭}}. ==Overview== The shrine's origins trace back to the transfer of the {{ill|Rokugō Shrine|ja|六郷神社}} deity in {{ill|Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|ja|大田区東六郷}}, Tokyo, by local residents moving to {{ill|Daishikawara reclaimed land|ja|大師河原干拓}}. To honor the achievements of Emperor Nintoku in river management of the Yodo River, they enshrined him as a guardian deity. Although the exact date of the shrine’s foundation is unclear, records such as the land survey-based {{ill|Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|ja|小田原衆所領役帳}} from Eiroku 2 (1559) first mention the shrine receiving three koku of red-seal land. Initially subordinate to the {{ill|Heikenji|ja|平間寺}} (Kawasaki Daishi), the shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} policy of the Meiji era, with a priest from Heikenji’s 38th head, Saeki Takamoto, serving as its chief priest. ==Festivals== During {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}, the shrine hosts a {{ill|Setsubun|ja|豆まき|lt=Bean-Throwing Festival}}, featuring not only beans but also sweets and rice crackers, attracting many children. In 1994, the shrine initiated an event during the Setsubun Festival where fortune beans included tickets for seaweed used in {{ill|Ehomaki|ja|恵方巻}}, and {{ill|Toshiotoko|ja|年男}} and {{ill|Toshionna|ja|年女}} participants ate Ehomaki facing the year's lucky direction. This was reportedly the first public Ehomaki event in the Kanto region. Additionally, as Daishikawara village was the discovery site of the {{ill|Nagajūrō Pear|ja|長十郎梨}}, the shrine holds a Nagajūrō Pear Harvest Festival. ==Sub-Shrines== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} - Known for the Kanamara Matsuri. Originally located near Daishi Station. Includes the {{ill|Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|伊勢社}} and an Inari shrine. * {{ill|Fujimori Inari Shrine|ja|藤森稲荷神社}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社}} - Hosts the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival in November. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} - Located within the Local History Museum. The precinct also houses the Local History Museum (on the second floor of the shrine office) and Wakamiya Kindergarten. ==Cultural Properties== * '''Wakamiya Hachimangu Kagura:''' A type of {{ill|Hayashi Kagura|ja|囃子神楽}} performed on the grounds. Designated as a {{ill|Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|ja|川崎市地域文化財}} in November 2018.[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Decision on Kawasaki City’s First Regional Cultural Properties (Kawasaki City Board of Education)] ==Parish Districts== The shrine’s parish covers the following areas: * Former {{ill|Kawasaki Town|ja|川崎町}}: * Former Kuranezaki and Zōshiki Villages: {{ill|Suzuki Town|ja|鈴木町}} * Former Sunako Town: {{ill|Ekimoto Town|ja|駅前本町}} * Former {{ill|Daishi Town|ja|大師町}}: * Former Daishikawara Village: Ise Town, Kannon, Shōwa, Daishikawara, Dai Town, Fujisaki * Former Kawanakashima Village: Kawanakashima, Kannon, Daishi Ekimae, Daishi Honmachi, Daishi Town, Higashimonzen * Former Inarishinden: Egawa, {{ill|Kojima Town|ja|小島町}}, Shiohama, Shōwa, Tamachi, Dekino, {{ill|Tonomachi|ja|殿町}}, Hinode, Yotsuya Kami-machi, Yotsuya Shimo-machi * Former Ikegami Shinden: Ikegami Shin-machi, Ikegami Town * Former {{ill|Tajima Town|ja|田島町}}: * Former Ōshima Village: Asano Town * Former Kodachi Landfill: Ōkawa Town, Shiroishi Town * Reclaimed Land: {{ill|Ukishima Town|ja|浮島町}}, {{ill|Ogishima|ja|扇島}}, {{ill|Chidori Town|ja|千鳥町}}, Nakase, {{ill|Higashi Ogishima|ja|東扇島}}, Mizue Town, Yakō ==Lineage of Chief Priests== {| class="wikitable" ! Generation !! Chief Priest |- | First || Nakamura Kazuhiko |- | Second || Nakamura Hiroken |- | Third || Nakamura Hiroko |- | Fourth || Nakamura Hirohiko |- | Fifth || Nakamura Kimiko |- | Sixth || Nakamura Hiroyuki |} ==Associated Shrines== * {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|厳島神社}} (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * {{ill|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)|ja|稲荷神社}} (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * {{ill|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|神明神社}} (Kawanakashima and Shiohama). ==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi Station|ja|川崎大師駅}} on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス}} Kawasai 23 line. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==See Also== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachimangu (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮}} ==External Links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20051218123032/http://tomuraya.co.jp/wakamiya.htm Wakamiya Hachimangu Official Site] - Archived on January 27, 2020. * [https://www.facebook.com/wakamiyasama/ Wakamiya Hachimangu] - Facebook * [http://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/osirase/sigaku/shuhou/seido/meibo/kawasakiku.html Kawasaki City Religious Corporation Registry] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B7%9D%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]]

Includes the {{ill|Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|伊勢社}} and an Inari shrine. * {{ill|Fujimori Inari Shrine|ja|藤森稲荷神社}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社}} - Hosts the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival in November. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} - Located within the Local History Museum. The precinct also houses the Local History Museum (on the second floor of the shrine office) and Wakamiya Kindergarten. ==Cultural Properties== * '''Wakamiya Hachimangu Kagura:''' A type of {{ill|Hayashi Kagura|ja|囃子神楽}} performed on the grounds. Designated as a {{ill|Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|ja|川崎市地域文化財}} in November 2018.[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Decision on Kawasaki City’s First Regional Cultural Properties (Kawasaki City Board of Education)] ==Parish Districts== The shrine’s parish covers the following areas: * Former {{ill|Kawasaki Town|ja|川崎町}}: * Former Kuranezaki and Zōshiki Villages: {{ill|Suzuki Town|ja|鈴木町}} * Former Sunako Town: {{ill|Ekimoto Town|ja|駅前本町}} * Former {{ill|Daishi Town|ja|大師町}}: * Former Daishikawara Village: Ise Town, Kannon, Shōwa, Daishikawara, Dai Town, Fujisaki * Former Kawanakashima Village: Kawanakashima, Kannon, Daishi Ekimae, Daishi Honmachi, Daishi Town, Higashimonzen * Former Inarishinden: Egawa, {{ill|Kojima Town|ja|小島町}}, Shiohama, Shōwa, Tamachi, Dekino, {{ill|Tonomachi|ja|殿町}}, Hinode, Yotsuya Kami-machi, Yotsuya Shimo-machi * Former Ikegami Shinden: Ikegami Shin-machi, Ikegami Town * Former {{ill|Tajima Town|ja|田島町}}: * Former Ōshima Village: Asano Town * Former Kodachi Landfill: Ōkawa Town, Shiroishi Town * Reclaimed Land: {{ill|Ukishima Town|ja|浮島町}}, {{ill|Ogishima|ja|扇島}}, {{ill|Chidori Town|ja|千鳥町}}, Nakase, {{ill|Higashi Ogishima|ja|東扇島}}, Mizue Town, Yakō ==Lineage of Chief Priests== {| class="wikitable" ! Generation !! Chief Priest |- | First || Nakamura Kazuhiko |- | Second || Nakamura Hiroken |- | Third || Nakamura Hiroko |- | Fourth || Nakamura Hirohiko |- | Fifth || Nakamura Kimiko |- | Sixth || Nakamura Hiroyuki |} ==Associated Shrines== * {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|厳島神社}} (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * {{ill|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)|ja|稲荷神社}} (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * {{ill|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|神明神社}} (Kawanakashima and Shiohama). ==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi Station|ja|川崎大師駅}} on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス}} Kawasai 23 line. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==See Also== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachimangu (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮}} ==External Links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20051218123032/http://tomuraya.co.jp/wakamiya.htm Wakamiya Hachimangu Official Site] - Archived on January 27, 2020. * [https://www.facebook.com/wakamiyasama/ Wakamiya Hachimangu] - Facebook * [http://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/osirase/sigaku/shuhou/seido/meibo/kawasakiku.html Kawasaki City Religious Corporation Registry] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B7%9D%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]]

{{Shrine | name=Wakamiya Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[image:Wakamiya Hachimangu 01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|4.7|N|139|43|28.7|E}}}} | location=Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki City, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan, Daishi Ekimae 2-chome 13-16 | Position={{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=32|lat_sec=4.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=43|lon_sec=28.7|label=Wakamiya Hachimangu|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | deity={{ill|Nintoku-tennō|ja|仁徳天皇}} | Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrines under the modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度#諸社}} | Foundation=Around {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 2 (1559) | festival= }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangu''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Daishi Ekimae|ja|大師駅前}}, {{ill|Kawasaki-ku|ja|川崎区}}, {{ill|Kawasaki City|ja|川崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It served as the guardian shrine for the former {{ill|Daishikawara|ja|大師河原}} area. Its precincts include the {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}}, which is famous for the {{ill|Kanamara Matsuri|ja|かなまら祭}}. ==Overview== The shrine's origins trace back to the transfer of the {{ill|Rokugō Shrine|ja|六郷神社}} deity in {{ill|Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|ja|大田区東六郷}}, Tokyo, by local residents moving to {{ill|Daishikawara reclaimed land|ja|大師河原干拓}}. To honor the achievements of Emperor Nintoku in river management of the Yodo River, they enshrined him as a guardian deity. Although the exact date of the shrine’s foundation is unclear, records such as the land survey-based {{ill|Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|ja|小田原衆所領役帳}} from Eiroku 2 (1559) first mention the shrine receiving three koku of red-seal land. Initially subordinate to the {{ill|Heikenji|ja|平間寺}} (Kawasaki Daishi), the shrine became independent during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} policy of the Meiji era, with a priest from Heikenji’s 38th head, Saeki Takamoto, serving as its chief priest. ==Festivals== During {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}, the shrine hosts a {{ill|Setsubun|ja|豆まき|lt=Bean-Throwing Festival}}, featuring not only beans but also sweets and rice crackers, attracting many children. In 1994, the shrine initiated an event during the Setsubun Festival where fortune beans included tickets for seaweed used in {{ill|Ehomaki|ja|恵方巻}}, and {{ill|Toshiotoko|ja|年男}} and {{ill|Toshionna|ja|年女}} participants ate Ehomaki facing the year's lucky direction. This was reportedly the first public Ehomaki event in the Kanto region. Additionally, as Daishikawara village was the discovery site of the {{ill|Nagajūrō Pear|ja|長十郎梨}}, the shrine holds a Nagajūrō Pear Harvest Festival. ==Sub-Shrines== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} - Known for the Kanamara Matsuri. Originally located near Daishi Station. Includes the {{ill|Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|伊勢社}} and an Inari shrine. * {{ill|Fujimori Inari Shrine|ja|藤森稲荷神社}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社}} - Hosts the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival in November. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} - Located within the Local History Museum. The precinct also houses the Local History Museum (on the second floor of the shrine office) and Wakamiya Kindergarten. ==Cultural Properties== * '''Wakamiya Hachimangu Kagura:''' A type of {{ill|Hayashi Kagura|ja|囃子神楽}} performed on the grounds. Designated as a {{ill|Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|ja|川崎市地域文化財}} in November 2018.[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Decision on Kawasaki City’s First Regional Cultural Properties (Kawasaki City Board of Education)] ==Parish Districts== The shrine’s parish covers the following areas: * Former {{ill|Kawasaki Town|ja|川崎町}}: * Former Kuranezaki and Zōshiki Villages: {{ill|Suzuki Town|ja|鈴木町}} * Former Sunako Town: {{ill|Ekimoto Town|ja|駅前本町}} * Former {{ill|Daishi Town|ja|大師町}}: * Former Daishikawara Village: Ise Town, Kannon, Shōwa, Daishikawara, Dai Town, Fujisaki * Former Kawanakashima Village: Kawanakashima, Kannon, Daishi Ekimae, Daishi Honmachi, Daishi Town, Higashimonzen * Former Inarishinden: Egawa, {{ill|Kojima Town|ja|小島町}}, Shiohama, Shōwa, Tamachi, Dekino, {{ill|Tonomachi|ja|殿町}}, Hinode, Yotsuya Kami-machi, Yotsuya Shimo-machi * Former Ikegami Shinden: Ikegami Shin-machi, Ikegami Town * Former {{ill|Tajima Town|ja|田島町}}: * Former Ōshima Village: Asano Town * Former Kodachi Landfill: Ōkawa Town, Shiroishi Town * Reclaimed Land: {{ill|Ukishima Town|ja|浮島町}}, {{ill|Ogishima|ja|扇島}}, {{ill|Chidori Town|ja|千鳥町}}, Nakase, {{ill|Higashi Ogishima|ja|東扇島}}, Mizue Town, Yakō ==Lineage of Chief Priests== {| class="wikitable" ! Generation !! Chief Priest |- | First || Nakamura Kazuhiko |- | Second || Nakamura Hiroken |- | Third || Nakamura Hiroko |- | Fourth || Nakamura Hirohiko |- | Fifth || Nakamura Kimiko |- | Sixth || Nakamura Hiroyuki |} ==Associated Shrines== * {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|厳島神社}} (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * {{ill|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)|ja|稲荷神社}} (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * {{ill|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|神明神社}} (Kawanakashima and Shiohama). ==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of {{ill|Kawasaki Daishi Station|ja|川崎大師駅}} on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス}} Kawasai 23 line. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==See Also== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachimangu (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮}} ==External Links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20051218123032/http://tomuraya.co.jp/wakamiya.htm Wakamiya Hachimangu Official Site] - Archived on January 27, 2020. * [https://www.facebook.com/wakamiyasama/ Wakamiya Hachimangu] - Facebook * [http://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/osirase/sigaku/shuhou/seido/meibo/kawasakiku.html Kawasaki City Religious Corporation Registry] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki-ku]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B7%9D%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]]
