revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616250]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine | name=Wakamiya Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|4.7|N|139|43|28.7|E}}}} | location=Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki City, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan, Daishi Ekimae 2-chome 13-16 | Position={{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=32|lat_sec=4.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=43|lon_sec=28.7|label=Wakamiya Hachimangu|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | deity=[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Nintoku-tennō]] | Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrines under the modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrines under the modern shrine Ranking system|qq=}} | Foundation=Around [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 2 (1559) | festival= }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangu''' (若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Daishi Ekimae|ja|大師駅前|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi Ekimae|qq=}}, [[:en:Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki|Kawasaki-ku]], [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It served as the guardian shrine for the former {{ill|Daishikawara|ja|大師河原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishikawara|qq=}} area. Its precincts include the {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}}, which is famous for the [[:en:Kanamara Matsuri|Kanamara Matsuri]]. ==Overview== The shrine's origins trace back to the transfer of the {{ill|Rokugō Shrine|ja|六郷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokugō Shrine|qq=}} deity in {{ill|Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|ja|大田区東六郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tokyo, by local residents moving to {{ill|Daishikawara reclaimed land|ja|大師河原干拓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishikawara reclaimed land|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. To honor the achievements of Emperor Nintoku in river management of the Yodo River, they enshrined him as a guardian deity. Although the exact date of the shrine’s foundation is unclear, records such as the land survey-based {{ill|Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|ja|小田原衆所領役帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|qq=}} from Eiroku 2 (1559) first mention the shrine receiving three koku of red-seal land. Initially subordinate to the [[:en:Kawasaki Daishi|Heikenji]] (Kawasaki Daishi), the shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] policy of the Meiji era, with a priest from Heikenji’s 38th head, Saeki Takamoto, serving as its chief priest. ==Festivals== During [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]], the shrine hosts a {{ill|Setsubun|ja|豆まき|lt=Bean-Throwing Festival|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}}, featuring not only beans but also sweets and rice crackers, attracting many children. In 1994, the shrine initiated an event during the Setsubun Festival where fortune beans included tickets for seaweed used in {{ill|Ehomaki|ja|恵方巻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehomaki|qq=}}, and {{ill|Toshiotoko|ja|年男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshiotoko|qq=}} and {{ill|Toshionna|ja|年女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshionna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年男・年女}} participants ate Ehomaki facing the year's lucky direction. This was reportedly the first public Ehomaki event in the Kanto region. Additionally, as Daishikawara village was the discovery site of the {{ill|Nagajūrō Pear|ja|長十郎梨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagajūrō Pear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine holds a Nagajūrō Pear Harvest Festival. ==Sub-Shrines== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} - Known for the Kanamara Matsuri. Originally located near Daishi Station. Includes the {{ill|Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|伊勢社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and an Inari shrine. * {{ill|Fujimori Inari Shrine|ja|藤森稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimori Inari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} - Hosts the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival in November. * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] - Located within the Local History Museum. The precinct also houses the Local History Museum (on the second floor of the shrine office) and Wakamiya Kindergarten. ==Cultural Properties== * '''Wakamiya Hachimangu Kagura:''' A type of {{ill|Hayashi Kagura|ja|囃子神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed on the grounds. Designated as a {{ill|Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|ja|川崎市地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|qq=}} in November 2018.[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Decision on Kawasaki City’s First Regional Cultural Properties (Kawasaki City Board of Education)] ==Parish Districts== The shrine’s parish covers the following areas: * Former {{ill|Kawasaki Town|ja|川崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Town|qq=}}: ** Former Kuranezaki and Zōshiki Villages: {{ill|Suzuki Town|ja|鈴木町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Town|qq=}} ** Former Sunako Town: {{ill|Ekimoto Town|ja|駅前本町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekimoto Town|qq=}} * Former {{ill|Daishi Town|ja|大師町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi Town|qq=}}: ** Former Daishikawara Village: Ise Town, Kannon, Shōwa, Daishikawara, Dai Town, Fujisaki ** Former Kawanakashima Village: Kawanakashima, Kannon, Daishi Ekimae, Daishi Honmachi, Daishi Town, Higashimonzen ** Former Inarishinden: Egawa, {{ill|Kojima Town|ja|小島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Town|qq=}}, Shiohama, Shōwa, Tamachi, Dekino, {{ill|Tonomachi|ja|殿町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonomachi|qq=}}, Hinode, Yotsuya Kami-machi, Yotsuya Shimo-machi ** Former Ikegami Shinden: Ikegami Shin-machi, Ikegami Town * Former {{ill|Tajima Town|ja|田島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Town|qq=}}: ** Former Ōshima Village: Asano Town ** Former Kodachi Landfill: Ōkawa Town, Shiroishi Town * Reclaimed Land: {{ill|Ukishima Town|ja|浮島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukishima Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Ogishima|ja|扇島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogishima|qq=}}, {{ill|Chidori Town|ja|千鳥町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chidori Town|qq=}}, Nakase, {{ill|Higashi Ogishima|ja|東扇島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Ogishima|qq=}}, Mizue Town, Yakō ==Lineage of Chief Priests== {| class="wikitable" ! Generation !! Chief Priest |- | First || Nakamura Kazuhiko |- | Second || Nakamura Hiroken |- | Third || Nakamura Hiroko |- | Fourth || Nakamura Hirohiko |- | Fifth || Nakamura Kimiko |- | Sixth || Nakamura Hiroyuki |} ==Associated Shrines== * {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)]] (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)]] (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] (Kawanakashima and Shiohama). ==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of [[:en:Kawasaki-Daishi Station|Kawasaki Daishi Station]] on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|qq=}} Kawasai 23 line. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==See Also== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachimangu (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachimangu (disambiguation)|qq=}} ==External Links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20051218123032/http://tomuraya.co.jp/wakamiya.htm Wakamiya Hachimangu Official Site] - Archived on January 27, 2020. * [https://www.facebook.com/wakamiyasama/ Wakamiya Hachimangu] - Facebook * [http://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/osirase/sigaku/shuhou/seido/meibo/kawasakiku.html Kawasaki City Religious Corporation Registry] {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (川崎市)|version=103645352|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Wakamiya Hachimangu 01.jpg|250px]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[sn:E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B7%9D%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]] [[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kawasaki]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (川崎市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Registered under the same name. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] (Kawanakashima and Shiohama). ==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of [[:en:Kawasaki-Daishi Station|Kawasaki Daishi Station]] on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|qq=}} Kawasai 23 line. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==See Also== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachimangu (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachimangu (disambiguation)|qq=}} ==External Links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20051218123032/http://tomuraya.co.jp/wakamiya.htm Wakamiya Hachimangu Official Site] - Archived on January 27, 2020. * [https://www.facebook.com/wakamiyasama/ Wakamiya Hachimangu] - Facebook * [http://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/osirase/sigaku/shuhou/seido/meibo/kawasakiku.html Kawasaki City Religious Corporation Registry] {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (川崎市)|version=103645352|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Wakamiya Hachimangu 01.jpg|250px]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[sn:E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B7%9D%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]] [[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kawasaki]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (川崎市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616250]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Wakamiya Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Wakamiya Hachimangu 01.jpg | location=Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki City, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan, Daishi Ekimae 2-chome 13-16 | Position={{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=32|lat_sec=4.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=43|lon_sec=28.7|label=Wakamiya Hachimangu|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | deity=[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Nintoku-tennō]] | Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrines under the modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrines under the modern shrine Ranking system|qq=}} | Foundation=Around [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 2 (1559) | festival= | prefecture={{ill|Kawasaki-ku|qid=Q1204949}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.53466111111111|N|139.7246638888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachimangu''' (若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Daishi Ekimae|ja|大師駅前|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi Ekimae|qq=}}, [[:en:Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki|Kawasaki-ku]], [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It served as the guardian shrine for the former {{ill|Daishikawara|ja|大師河原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishikawara|qq=}} area. Its precincts include the {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}}, which is famous for the [[:en:Kanamara Matsuri|Kanamara Matsuri]].
==Overview==
The shrine's origins trace back to the transfer of the {{ill|Rokugō Shrine|ja|六郷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokugō Shrine|qq=}} deity in {{ill|Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|ja|大田区東六郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tokyo, by local residents moving to {{ill|Daishikawara reclaimed land|ja|大師河原干拓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishikawara reclaimed land|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. To honor the achievements of Emperor Nintoku in river management of the Yodo River, they enshrined him as a guardian deity. Although the exact date of the shrine’s foundation is unclear, records such as the land survey-based {{ill|Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|ja|小田原衆所領役帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|qq=}} from Eiroku 2 (1559) first mention the shrine receiving three koku of red-seal land. Initially subordinate to the [[:en:Kawasaki Daishi|Heikenji]] (Kawasaki Daishi), the shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] policy of the Meiji era, with a priest from Heikenji’s 38th head, Saeki Takamoto, serving as its chief priest.
==Festivals==
During [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]], the shrine hosts a {{ill|Setsubun|ja|豆まき|lt=Bean-Throwing Festival|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}}, featuring not only beans but also sweets and rice crackers, attracting many children. In 1994, the shrine initiated an event during the Setsubun Festival where fortune beans included tickets for seaweed used in {{ill|Ehomaki|ja|恵方巻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehomaki|qq=}}, and {{ill|Toshiotoko|ja|年男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshiotoko|qq=}} and {{ill|Toshionna|ja|年女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshionna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年男・年女}} participants ate Ehomaki facing the year's lucky direction. This was reportedly the first public Ehomaki event in the Kanto region.
Additionally, as Daishikawara village was the discovery site of the {{ill|Nagajūrō Pear|ja|長十郎梨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagajūrō Pear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine holds a Nagajūrō Pear Harvest Festival.
==Sub-Shrines==
* {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} - Known for the Kanamara Matsuri. Originally located near Daishi Station. Includes the {{ill|Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|伊勢社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and an Inari shrine. * {{ill|Fujimori Inari Shrine|ja|藤森稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimori Inari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} - Hosts the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival in November. * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] - Located within the Local History Museum.
The precinct also houses the Local History Museum (on the second floor of the shrine office) and Wakamiya Kindergarten.
==Cultural Properties==
* '''Wakamiya Hachimangu Kagura:''' A type of {{ill|Hayashi Kagura|ja|囃子神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed on the grounds. Designated as a {{ill|Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|ja|川崎市地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|qq=}} in November 2018.<ref>[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Decision on Kawasaki City’s First Regional Cultural Properties (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]</ref>
==Parish Districts==
The shrine’s parish covers the following areas:
* Former {{ill|Kawasaki Town|ja|川崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Town|qq=}}: ** Former Kuranezaki and Zōshiki Villages: {{ill|Suzuki Town|ja|鈴木町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Town|qq=}} ** Former Sunako Town: {{ill|Ekimoto Town|ja|駅前本町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekimoto Town|qq=}} * Former {{ill|Daishi Town|ja|大師町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi Town|qq=}}: ** Former Daishikawara Village: Ise Town, Kannon, Shōwa, Daishikawara, Dai Town, Fujisaki ** Former Kawanakashima Village: Kawanakashima, Kannon, Daishi Ekimae, Daishi Honmachi, Daishi Town, Higashimonzen ** Former Inarishinden: Egawa, {{ill|Kojima Town|ja|小島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Town|qq=}}, Shiohama, Shōwa, Tamachi, Dekino, {{ill|Tonomachi|ja|殿町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonomachi|qq=}}, Hinode, Yotsuya Kami-machi, Yotsuya Shimo-machi ** Former Ikegami Shinden: Ikegami Shin-machi, Ikegami Town * Former {{ill|Tajima Town|ja|田島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Town|qq=}}: ** Former Ōshima Village: Asano Town ** Former Kodachi Landfill: Ōkawa Town, Shiroishi Town * Reclaimed Land: {{ill|Ukishima Town|ja|浮島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukishima Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Ogishima|ja|扇島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogishima|qq=}}, {{ill|Chidori Town|ja|千鳥町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chidori Town|qq=}}, Nakase, {{ill|Higashi Ogishima|ja|東扇島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Ogishima|qq=}}, Mizue Town, Yakō
==Lineage of Chief Priests==
{| class="wikitable" ! Generation !! Chief Priest |- | First || Nakamura Kazuhiko |- | Second || Nakamura Hiroken |- | Third || Nakamura Hiroko |- | Fourth || Nakamura Hirohiko |- | Fifth || Nakamura Kimiko |- | Sixth || Nakamura Hiroyuki |}
==Associated Shrines==
* {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)]] (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)]] (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] (Kawanakashima and Shiohama).
==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of [[:en:Kawasaki-Daishi Station|Kawasaki Daishi Station]] on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|qq=}} Kawasai 23 line.

==Associated Shrines==
* {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)]] (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)]] (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] (Kawanakashima and Shiohama).
==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of [[:en:Kawasaki-Daishi Station|Kawasaki Daishi Station]] on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|qq=}} Kawasai 23 line.

[[image:Wakamiya Hachimangu 01.jpg|250px]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[sn:E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B7%9D%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]] [[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]]

[[Category:Shinto shrines in Kawasaki]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (川崎市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616250]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Wakamiya Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Wakamiya Hachimangu 01.jpg | location=Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki City, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan, Daishi Ekimae 2-chome 13-16 | Position={{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=32|lat_sec=4.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=43|lon_sec=28.7|label=Wakamiya Hachimangu|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | deity=[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Nintoku-tennō]] | Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrines under the modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrines under the modern shrine Ranking system|qq=}} | Foundation=Around [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 2 (1559) | festival= | prefecture={{ill|Kawasaki-ku|qid=Q1204949}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.53466111111111|N|139.7246638888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachimangu''' (若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Daishi Ekimae|ja|大師駅前|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi Ekimae|qq=}}, [[:en:Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki|Kawasaki-ku]], [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It served as the guardian shrine for the former {{ill|Daishikawara|ja|大師河原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishikawara|qq=}} area. Its precincts include the {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}}, which is famous for the [[:en:Kanamara Matsuri|Kanamara Matsuri]].
==Overview==
The shrine's origins trace back to the transfer of the {{ill|Rokugō Shrine|ja|六郷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokugō Shrine|qq=}} deity in {{ill|Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|ja|大田区東六郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tokyo, by local residents moving to {{ill|Daishikawara reclaimed land|ja|大師河原干拓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishikawara reclaimed land|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. To honor the achievements of Emperor Nintoku in river management of the Yodo River, they enshrined him as a guardian deity. Although the exact date of the shrine’s foundation is unclear, records such as the land survey-based {{ill|Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|ja|小田原衆所領役帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|qq=}} from Eiroku 2 (1559) first mention the shrine receiving three koku of red-seal land. Initially subordinate to the [[:en:Kawasaki Daishi|Heikenji]] (Kawasaki Daishi), the shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] policy of the Meiji era, with a priest from Heikenji’s 38th head, Saeki Takamoto, serving as its chief priest.
==Festivals==
During [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]], the shrine hosts a {{ill|Setsubun|ja|豆まき|lt=Bean-Throwing Festival|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}}, featuring not only beans but also sweets and rice crackers, attracting many children. In 1994, the shrine initiated an event during the Setsubun Festival where fortune beans included tickets for seaweed used in {{ill|Ehomaki|ja|恵方巻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehomaki|qq=}}, and {{ill|Toshiotoko|ja|年男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshiotoko|qq=}} and {{ill|Toshionna|ja|年女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshionna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年男・年女}} participants ate Ehomaki facing the year's lucky direction. This was reportedly the first public Ehomaki event in the Kanto region.
Additionally, as Daishikawara village was the discovery site of the {{ill|Nagajūrō Pear|ja|長十郎梨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagajūrō Pear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine holds a Nagajūrō Pear Harvest Festival.
==Sub-Shrines==
* {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} - Known for the Kanamara Matsuri. Originally located near Daishi Station. Includes the {{ill|Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|伊勢社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and an Inari shrine. * {{ill|Fujimori Inari Shrine|ja|藤森稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimori Inari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} - Hosts the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival in November. * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] - Located within the Local History Museum.
The precinct also houses the Local History Museum (on the second floor of the shrine office) and Wakamiya Kindergarten.
==Cultural Properties==
* '''Wakamiya Hachimangu Kagura:''' A type of {{ill|Hayashi Kagura|ja|囃子神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed on the grounds. Designated as a {{ill|Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|ja|川崎市地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|qq=}} in November 2018.<ref>[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Decision on Kawasaki City’s First Regional Cultural Properties (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]</ref>
==Parish Districts==
The shrine’s parish covers the following areas:
* Former {{ill|Kawasaki Town|ja|川崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Town|qq=}}: ** Former Kuranezaki and Zōshiki Villages: {{ill|Suzuki Town|ja|鈴木町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Town|qq=}} ** Former Sunako Town: {{ill|Ekimoto Town|ja|駅前本町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekimoto Town|qq=}} * Former {{ill|Daishi Town|ja|大師町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi Town|qq=}}: ** Former Daishikawara Village: Ise Town, Kannon, Shōwa, Daishikawara, Dai Town, Fujisaki ** Former Kawanakashima Village: Kawanakashima, Kannon, Daishi Ekimae, Daishi Honmachi, Daishi Town, Higashimonzen ** Former Inarishinden: Egawa, {{ill|Kojima Town|ja|小島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Town|qq=}}, Shiohama, Shōwa, Tamachi, Dekino, {{ill|Tonomachi|ja|殿町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonomachi|qq=}}, Hinode, Yotsuya Kami-machi, Yotsuya Shimo-machi ** Former Ikegami Shinden: Ikegami Shin-machi, Ikegami Town * Former {{ill|Tajima Town|ja|田島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Town|qq=}}: ** Former Ōshima Village: Asano Town ** Former Kodachi Landfill: Ōkawa Town, Shiroishi Town * Reclaimed Land: {{ill|Ukishima Town|ja|浮島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukishima Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Ogishima|ja|扇島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogishima|qq=}}, {{ill|Chidori Town|ja|千鳥町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chidori Town|qq=}}, Nakase, {{ill|Higashi Ogishima|ja|東扇島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Ogishima|qq=}}, Mizue Town, Yakō
==Lineage of Chief Priests==

==Associated Shrines==
* {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)]] (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)]] (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] (Kawanakashima and Shiohama).
==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of [[:en:Kawasaki-Daishi Station|Kawasaki Daishi Station]] on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|qq=}} Kawasai 23 line.

on wikidata|WD=Q11576650}}: ** Former Ōshima Village: Asano Town ** Former Kodachi Landfill: Ōkawa Town, Shiroishi Town * Reclaimed Land: {{ill|Ukishima Town|ja|浮島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukishima Town|qq=|1=Ukishima Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864694}}, {{ill|Ogishima|ja|扇島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogishima|qq=|1=Ōgishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496835}}, {{ill|Chidori Town|ja|千鳥町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chidori Town|qq=|1=Chidori Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406909}}, Nakase, {{ill|Higashi Ogishima|ja|東扇島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Ogishima|qq=|1=Higashi-Ōgishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11527048}}, Mizue Town, Yakō
==Lineage of Chief Priests==
{| class="wikitable" ! Generation !! Chief Priest |- | First || Nakamura Kazuhiko |- | Second || Nakamura Hiroken |- | Third || Nakamura Hiroko |- | Fourth || Nakamura Hirohiko |- | Fifth || Nakamura Kimiko |- | Sixth || Nakamura Hiroyuki |}
==Associated Shrines==
* {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=|1=Suijinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55522725}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)]] (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)]] (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] (Kawanakashima and Shiohama).
==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of [[:en:Kawasaki-Daishi Station|Kawasaki Daishi Station]] on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|qq=|1=Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinko Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478611}} Kawasai 23 line.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11616250]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Wakamiya Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Wakamiya Hachimangu 01.jpg | location=Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki City, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan, Daishi Ekimae 2-chome 13-16 | Position={{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=32|lat_sec=4.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=43|lon_sec=28.7|label=Wakamiya Hachimangu|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | deity=[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Nintoku-tennō]] | Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrines under the modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrines under the modern shrine Ranking system|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Foundation=Around [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 2 (1559) | festival= | prefecture={{ill|Kawasaki-ku|qid=Q1204949}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.53466111111111|N|139.7246638888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachimangu''' (若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Daishi Ekimae|ja|大師駅前|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi Ekimae|qq=|1=Daishi-ekimae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110476046}}, [[:en:Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki|Kawasaki-ku]], [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It served as the guardian shrine for the former {{ill|Daishikawara|ja|大師河原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishikawara|qq=|1=Daishi-gawara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110476158}} area. Its precincts include the {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=|1=Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647006}}, which is famous for the [[:en:Kanamara Matsuri|Kanamara Matsuri]].
==Overview==
The shrine's origins trace back to the transfer of the {{ill|Rokugō Shrine|ja|六郷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokugō Shrine|qq=}} deity in {{ill|Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|ja|大田区東六郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tokyo, by local residents moving to {{ill|Daishikawara reclaimed land|ja|大師河原干拓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishikawara reclaimed land|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. To honor the achievements of Emperor Nintoku in river management of the Yodo River, they enshrined him as a guardian deity. Although the exact date of the shrine’s foundation is unclear, records such as the land survey-based {{ill|Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|ja|小田原衆所領役帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|qq=|1=Odawara-shū Shoryō Yakuchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463001}} from Eiroku 2 (1559) first mention the shrine receiving three koku of red-seal land. Initially subordinate to the [[:en:Kawasaki Daishi|Heikenji]] (Kawasaki Daishi), the shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] policy of the Meiji era, with a priest from Heikenji’s 38th head, Saeki Takamoto, serving as its chief priest.
==Festivals==
During [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]], the shrine hosts a {{ill|Setsubun|ja|豆まき|lt=Bean-Throwing Festival|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}}, featuring not only beans but also sweets and rice crackers, attracting many children. In 1994, the shrine initiated an event during the Setsubun Festival where fortune beans included tickets for seaweed used in {{ill|Ehomaki|ja|恵方巻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehomaki|qq=|1=ehōmaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4389530}}, and {{ill|Toshiotoko|ja|年男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshiotoko|qq=|1=Toshiotoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483865}} and {{ill|Toshionna|ja|年女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshionna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年男・年女|1=Toshionna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483867}} participants ate Ehomaki facing the year's lucky direction. This was reportedly the first public Ehomaki event in the Kanto region.
Additionally, as Daishikawara village was the discovery site of the {{ill|Nagajūrō Pear|ja|長十郎梨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagajūrō Pear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine holds a Nagajūrō Pear Harvest Festival.
==Sub-Shrines==
* {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=|1=Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647006}} - Known for the Kanamara Matsuri. Originally located near Daishi Station. Includes the {{ill|Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|伊勢社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and an Inari shrine. * {{ill|Fujimori Inari Shrine|ja|藤森稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimori Inari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} - Hosts the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival in November. * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] - Located within the Local History Museum.
The precinct also houses the Local History Museum (on the second floor of the shrine office) and Wakamiya Kindergarten.
==Cultural Properties==
* '''Wakamiya Hachimangu Kagura:''' A type of {{ill|Hayashi Kagura|ja|囃子神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed on the grounds. Designated as a {{ill|Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|ja|川崎市地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|qq=|1=Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65265924}} in November 2018.<ref>[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Decision on Kawasaki City’s First Regional Cultural Properties (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]</ref>
==Parish Districts==
The shrine’s parish covers the following areas:
* Former {{ill|Kawasaki Town|ja|川崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Town|qq=|1=Kawasakimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31299}}: ** Former Kuranezaki and Zōshiki Villages: {{ill|Suzuki Town|ja|鈴木町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Town|qq=|1=Suzuki Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11649084}} ** Former Sunako Town: {{ill|Ekimoto Town|ja|駅前本町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekimoto Town|qq=|1=Ekimoto Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123408268}} * Former {{ill|Daishi Town|ja|大師町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi Town|qq=|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435241}}: ** Former Daishikawara Village: Ise Town, Kannon, Shōwa, Daishikawara, Dai Town, Fujisaki ** Former Kawanakashima Village: Kawanakashima, Kannon, Daishi Ekimae, Daishi Honmachi, Daishi Town, Higashimonzen ** Former Inarishinden: Egawa, {{ill|Kojima Town|ja|小島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Town|qq=|1=Oshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460195}}, Shiohama, Shōwa, Tamachi, Dekino, {{ill|Tonomachi|ja|殿町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonomachi|qq=|1=Tonomachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45344685}}, Hinode, Yotsuya Kami-machi, Yotsuya Shimo-machi ** Former Ikegami Shinden: Ikegami Shin-machi, Ikegami Town * Former {{ill|Tajima Town|ja|田島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Town|qq=|1=Tajima Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576650}}: ** Former Ōshima Village: Asano Town ** Former Kodachi Landfill: Ōkawa Town, Shiroishi Town * Reclaimed Land: {{ill|Ukishima Town|ja|浮島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukishima Town|qq=|1=Ukishima Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864694}}, {{ill|Ogishima|ja|扇島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogishima|qq=|1=Ōgishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496835}}, {{ill|Chidori Town|ja|千鳥町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chidori Town|qq=|1=Chidori Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406909}}, Nakase, {{ill|Higashi Ogishima|ja|東扇島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Ogishima|qq=|1=Higashi-Ōgishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11527048}}, Mizue Town, Yakō
==Lineage of Chief Priests==
{| class="wikitable" ! Generation !! Chief Priest |- | First || Nakamura Kazuhiko |- | Second || Nakamura Hiroken |- | Third || Nakamura Hiroko |- | Fourth || Nakamura Hirohiko |- | Fifth || Nakamura Kimiko |- | Sixth || Nakamura Hiroyuki |}
==Associated Shrines==
* {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=|1=Suijinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55522725}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)]] (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)]] (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] (Kawanakashima and Shiohama).
==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of [[:en:Kawasaki-Daishi Station|Kawasaki Daishi Station]] on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|qq=|1=Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinko Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478611}} Kawasai 23 line.
==References== {{Reflist}}
==See Also== * {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=|1=Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647006}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachimangu (disambiguation)|ja|若宮八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachimangu (disambiguation)|qq=|1=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616249}}

Its precincts include the {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=|1=Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647006}}, which is famous for the [[:en:Kanamara Matsuri|Kanamara Matsuri]].

Although the exact date of the shrine’s foundation is unclear, records such as the land survey-based {{ill|Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|ja|小田原衆所領役帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|qq=|1=Odawara-shū Shoryō Yakuchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463001}} from Eiroku 2 (1559) first mention the shrine receiving three koku of red-seal land. ==Festivals==
During [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]], the shrine hosts a {{ill|Setsubun|ja|豆まき|lt=Bean-Throwing Festival|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}}, featuring not only beans but also sweets and rice crackers, attracting many children. In 1994, the shrine initiated an event during the Setsubun Festival where fortune beans included tickets for seaweed used in {{ill|Ehomaki|ja|恵方巻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehomaki|qq=|1=ehōmaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4389530}}, and {{ill|Toshiotoko|ja|年男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshiotoko|qq=|1=Toshiotoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483865}} and {{ill|Toshionna|ja|年女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshionna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年男・年女|1=Toshionna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483867}} participants ate Ehomaki facing the year's lucky direction. This was reportedly the first public Ehomaki event in the Kanto region.
Additionally, as Daishikawara village was the discovery site of the {{ill|Nagajūrō Pear|ja|長十郎梨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagajūrō Pear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine holds a Nagajūrō Pear Harvest Festival.
==Sub-Shrines==
* {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=|1=Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647006}} - Known for the Kanamara Matsuri. Originally located near Daishi Station. Includes the {{ill|Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|伊勢社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and an Inari shrine. * {{ill|Fujimori Inari Shrine|ja|藤森稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimori Inari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} - Hosts the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival in November. * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] - Located within the Local History Museum.
The precinct also houses the Local History Museum (on the second floor of the shrine office) and Wakamiya Kindergarten.
==Cultural Properties==
* '''Wakamiya Hachimangu Kagura:''' A type of {{ill|Hayashi Kagura|ja|囃子神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed on the grounds. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kawasaki]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (川崎市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kawasaki)]][[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11616250]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Wakamiya Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Wakamiya Hachimangu 01.jpg | location=Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki City, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan, Daishi Ekimae 2-chome 13-16 | Position={{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=32|lat_sec=4.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=43|lon_sec=28.7|label=Wakamiya Hachimangu|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | deity=[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Nintoku-tennō]] | Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrines under the modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrines under the modern shrine Ranking system|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Foundation=Around [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 2 (1559) | festival= | prefecture={{ill|Kawasaki-ku|qid=Q1204949}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.53466111111111|N|139.7246638888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachimangu''' (若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Daishi Ekimae|ja|大師駅前|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi Ekimae|qq=|1=Daishi-ekimae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110476046}}, [[:en:Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki|Kawasaki-ku]], [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It served as the guardian shrine for the former {{ill|Daishikawara|ja|大師河原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishikawara|qq=|1=Daishi-gawara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110476158}} area. Its precincts include the {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=|1=Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647006}}, which is famous for the [[:en:Kanamara Matsuri|Kanamara Matsuri]].
==Overview==
The shrine's origins trace back to the transfer of the {{ill|Rokugō Shrine|ja|六郷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokugō Shrine|qq=}} deity in {{ill|Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|ja|大田区東六郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tokyo, by local residents moving to {{ill|Daishikawara reclaimed land|ja|大師河原干拓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishikawara reclaimed land|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. To honor the achievements of Emperor Nintoku in river management of the Yodo River, they enshrined him as a guardian deity. Although the exact date of the shrine’s foundation is unclear, records such as the land survey-based {{ill|Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|ja|小田原衆所領役帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|qq=|1=Odawara-shū Shoryō Yakuchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463001}} from Eiroku 2 (1559) first mention the shrine receiving three koku of red-seal land. Initially subordinate to the [[:en:Kawasaki Daishi|Heikenji]] (Kawasaki Daishi), the shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] policy of the Meiji era, with a priest from Heikenji’s 38th head, Saeki Takamoto, serving as its chief priest.
==Festivals==
During [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]], the shrine hosts a {{ill|Setsubun|ja|豆まき|lt=Bean-Throwing Festival|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}}, featuring not only beans but also sweets and rice crackers, attracting many children. In 1994, the shrine initiated an event during the Setsubun Festival where fortune beans included tickets for seaweed used in {{ill|Ehomaki|ja|恵方巻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehomaki|qq=|1=ehōmaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4389530}}, and {{ill|Toshiotoko|ja|年男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshiotoko|qq=|1=Toshiotoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483865}} and {{ill|Toshionna|ja|年女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshionna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年男・年女|1=Toshionna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483867}} participants ate Ehomaki facing the year's lucky direction. This was reportedly the first public Ehomaki event in the Kanto region.
Additionally, as Daishikawara village was the discovery site of the {{ill|Nagajūrō Pear|ja|長十郎梨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagajūrō Pear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine holds a Nagajūrō Pear Harvest Festival.
==Sub-Shrines==
* {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=|1=Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647006}} - Known for the Kanamara Matsuri. Originally located near Daishi Station. Includes the {{ill|Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|伊勢社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and an Inari shrine. * {{ill|Fujimori Inari Shrine|ja|藤森稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimori Inari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} - Hosts the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival in November. * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] - Located within the Local History Museum.
The precinct also houses the Local History Museum (on the second floor of the shrine office) and Wakamiya Kindergarten.
==Cultural Properties==
* '''Wakamiya Hachimangu Kagura:''' A type of {{ill|Hayashi Kagura|ja|囃子神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed on the grounds. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11616250]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Wakamiya Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Wakamiya Hachimangu 01.jpg | location=Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki City, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan, Daishi Ekimae 2-chome 13-16 | Position={{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=32|lat_sec=4.7|lon_deg=139|lon_min=43|lon_sec=28.7|label=Wakamiya Hachimangu|position=left|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | deity=[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Nintoku-tennō]] | Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrines under the modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrines under the modern shrine Ranking system|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Foundation=Around [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 2 (1559) | festival= | prefecture={{ill|Kawasaki-ku|qid=Q1204949}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.53466111111111|N|139.7246638888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachimangu''' (若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Daishi Ekimae|ja|大師駅前|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi Ekimae|qq=|1=Daishi-ekimae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110476046}}, [[:en:Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki|Kawasaki-ku]], [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It served as the guardian shrine for the former {{ill|Daishikawara|ja|大師河原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishikawara|qq=|1=Daishi-gawara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110476158}} area. Its precincts include the {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=|1=Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647006}}, which is famous for the [[:en:Kanamara Matsuri|Kanamara Matsuri]].
==Overview==
The shrine's origins trace back to the transfer of the {{ill|Rokugō Shrine|ja|六郷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokugō Shrine|qq=}} deity in {{ill|Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|ja|大田区東六郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tokyo, by local residents moving to {{ill|Daishikawara reclaimed land|ja|大師河原干拓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishikawara reclaimed land|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. To honor the achievements of Emperor Nintoku in river management of the Yodo River, they enshrined him as a guardian deity. Although the exact date of the shrine’s foundation is unclear, records such as the land survey-based {{ill|Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|ja|小田原衆所領役帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|qq=|1=Odawara-shū Shoryō Yakuchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463001}} from Eiroku 2 (1559) first mention the shrine receiving three koku of red-seal land. Initially subordinate to the [[:en:Kawasaki Daishi|Heikenji]] (Kawasaki Daishi), the shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] policy of the Meiji era, with a priest from Heikenji’s 38th head, Saeki Takamoto, serving as its chief priest.
==Festivals==
During [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]], the shrine hosts a {{ill|Setsubun|ja|豆まき|lt=Bean-Throwing Festival|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}}, featuring not only beans but also sweets and rice crackers, attracting many children. In 1994, the shrine initiated an event during the Setsubun Festival where fortune beans included tickets for seaweed used in {{ill|Ehomaki|ja|恵方巻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehomaki|qq=|1=ehōmaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4389530}}, and {{ill|Toshiotoko|ja|年男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshiotoko|qq=|1=Toshiotoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483865}} and {{ill|Toshionna|ja|年女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshionna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年男・年女|1=Toshionna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483867}} participants ate Ehomaki facing the year's lucky direction. This was reportedly the first public Ehomaki event in the Kanto region.
Additionally, as Daishikawara village was the discovery site of the {{ill|Nagajūrō Pear|ja|長十郎梨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagajūrō Pear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine holds a Nagajūrō Pear Harvest Festival.
==Sub-Shrines==
* {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=|1=Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647006}} - Known for the Kanamara Matsuri. Originally located near Daishi Station. Includes the {{ill|Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|伊勢社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and an Inari shrine. * {{ill|Fujimori Inari Shrine|ja|藤森稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimori Inari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} - Hosts the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival in November. * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] - Located within the Local History Museum.
The precinct also houses the Local History Museum (on the second floor of the shrine office) and Wakamiya Kindergarten.
==Cultural Properties==
* '''Wakamiya Hachimangu Kagura:''' A type of {{ill|Hayashi Kagura|ja|囃子神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed on the grounds. Designated as a {{ill|Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|ja|川崎市地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|qq=|1=Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65265924}} in November 2018.<ref>[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Decision on Kawasaki City’s First Regional Cultural Properties (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]</ref>
==Parish Districts==
The shrine’s parish covers the following areas:
* Former {{ill|Kawasaki Town|ja|川崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Town|qq=|1=Kawasakimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31299}}: ** Former Kuranezaki and Zōshiki Villages: {{ill|Suzuki Town|ja|鈴木町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Town|qq=|1=Suzuki Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11649084}} ** Former Sunako Town: {{ill|Ekimoto Town|ja|駅前本町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekimoto Town|qq=|1=Ekimoto Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123408268}} * Former {{ill|Daishi Town|ja|大師町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi Town|qq=|1=Daishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435241}}: ** Former Daishikawara Village: Ise Town, Kannon, Shōwa, Daishikawara, Dai Town, Fujisaki ** Former Kawanakashima Village: Kawanakashima, Kannon, Daishi Ekimae, Daishi Honmachi, Daishi Town, Higashimonzen ** Former Inarishinden: Egawa, {{ill|Kojima Town|ja|小島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Town|qq=|1=Oshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460195}}, Shiohama, Shōwa, Tamachi, Dekino, {{ill|Tonomachi|ja|殿町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonomachi|qq=|1=Tonomachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45344685}}, Hinode, Yotsuya Kami-machi, Yotsuya Shimo-machi ** Former Ikegami Shinden: Ikegami Shin-machi, Ikegami Town * Former {{ill|Tajima Town|ja|田島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Town|qq=|1=Tajima Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576650}}: ** Former Ōshima Village: Asano Town ** Former Kodachi Landfill: Ōkawa Town, Shiroishi Town * Reclaimed Land: {{ill|Ukishima Town|ja|浮島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukishima Town|qq=|1=Ukishima Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864694}}, {{ill|Ogishima|ja|扇島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogishima|qq=|1=Ōgishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496835}}, {{ill|Chidori Town|ja|千鳥町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chidori Town|qq=|1=Chidori Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406909}}, Nakase, {{ill|Higashi Ogishima|ja|東扇島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Ogishima|qq=|1=Higashi-Ōgishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11527048}}, Mizue Town, Yakō
==Lineage of Chief Priests==
{| class="wikitable" ! Generation !! Chief Priest |- | First || Nakamura Kazuhiko |- | Second || Nakamura Hiroken |- | Third || Nakamura Hiroko |- | Fourth || Nakamura Hirohiko |- | Fifth || Nakamura Kimiko |- | Sixth || Nakamura Hiroyuki |}
==Associated Shrines==
* {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=|1=Suijinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55522725}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)]] (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)]] (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] (Kawanakashima and Shiohama).
==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of [[:en:Kawasaki-Daishi Station|Kawasaki Daishi Station]] on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|qq=|1=Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinko Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478611}} Kawasai 23 line.

'''Wakamiya Hachimangu''' (若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Daishi Ekimae|ja|大師駅前|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishi Ekimae|qq=|1=Daishi-ekimae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110476046}}, [[:en:Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki|Kawasaki-ku]], [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It served as the guardian shrine for the former {{ill|Daishikawara|ja|大師河原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishikawara|qq=|1=Daishi-gawara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110476158}} area. Its precincts include the {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=|1=Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647006}}, which is famous for the [[:en:Kanamara Matsuri|Kanamara Matsuri]].
==Overview==
The shrine's origins trace back to the transfer of the {{ill|Rokugō Shrine|ja|六郷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokugō Shrine|qq=}} deity in {{ill|Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|ja|大田区東六郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashirokugō, Ōta Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tokyo, by local residents moving to {{ill|Daishikawara reclaimed land|ja|大師河原干拓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishikawara reclaimed land|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. To honor the achievements of Emperor Nintoku in river management of the Yodo River, they enshrined him as a guardian deity. Although the exact date of the shrine’s foundation is unclear, records such as the land survey-based {{ill|Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|ja|小田原衆所領役帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odawara Shūryō Yakuchō|qq=|1=Odawara-shū Shoryō Yakuchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463001}} from Eiroku 2 (1559) first mention the shrine receiving three koku of red-seal land. Initially subordinate to the [[:en:Kawasaki Daishi|Heikenji]] (Kawasaki Daishi), the shrine became independent during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] policy of the Meiji era, with a priest from Heikenji’s 38th head, Saeki Takamoto, serving as its chief priest.
==Festivals==
During [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]], the shrine hosts a {{ill|Setsubun|ja|豆まき|lt=Bean-Throwing Festival|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}}, featuring not only beans but also sweets and rice crackers, attracting many children. In 1994, the shrine initiated an event during the Setsubun Festival where fortune beans included tickets for seaweed used in {{ill|Ehomaki|ja|恵方巻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehomaki|qq=|1=ehōmaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4389530}}, and {{ill|Toshiotoko|ja|年男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshiotoko|qq=|1=Toshiotoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483865}} and {{ill|Toshionna|ja|年女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshionna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年男・年女|1=Toshionna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483867}} participants ate Ehomaki facing the year's lucky direction. This was reportedly the first public Ehomaki event in the Kanto region.
Additionally, as Daishikawara village was the discovery site of the {{ill|Nagajūrō Pear|ja|長十郎梨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagajūrō Pear|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine holds a Nagajūrō Pear Harvest Festival.
==Sub-Shrines==
* {{ill|Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|金山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=|1=Kanayama Shrine (Kawasaki City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647006}} - Known for the Kanamara Matsuri. Originally located near Daishi Station. Includes the {{ill|Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|ja|伊勢社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Shrine (Kawasaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and an Inari shrine. * {{ill|Fujimori Inari Shrine|ja|藤森稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimori Inari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} - Hosts the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival in November. * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] - Located within the Local History Museum.
The precinct also houses the Local History Museum (on the second floor of the shrine office) and Wakamiya Kindergarten.
==Cultural Properties==
* '''Wakamiya Hachimangu Kagura:''' A type of {{ill|Hayashi Kagura|ja|囃子神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed on the grounds. Designated as a {{ill|Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|ja|川崎市地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|qq=|1=Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65265924}} in November 2018.<ref>[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Decision on Kawasaki City’s First Regional Cultural Properties (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]</ref>
==Parish Districts==
The shrine’s parish covers the following areas:
* Former {{ill|Kawasaki Town|ja|川崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Town|qq=|1=Kawasakimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31299}}: ** Former Kuranezaki and ** Former Ikegami Shinden: Ikegami Shin-machi, Ikegami Town * Former {{ill|Tajima Town|ja|田島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Town|qq=|1=Tajima Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576650}}: ** Former Ōshima Village: Asano Town ** Former Kodachi Landfill: Ōkawa Town, Shiroishi Town * Reclaimed Land: {{ill|Ukishima Town|ja|浮島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukishima Town|qq=|1=Ukishima Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864694}}, {{ill|Ogishima|ja|扇島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogishima|qq=|1=Ōgishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496835}}, {{ill|Chidori Town|ja|千鳥町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chidori Town|qq=|1=Chidori Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406909}}, Nakase, {{ill|Higashi Ogishima|ja|東扇島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Ogishima|qq=|1=Higashi-Ōgishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11527048}}, Mizue Town, Yakō
==Lineage of Chief Priests==
{| class="wikitable" ! Generation !! Chief Priest |- | First || Nakamura Kazuhiko |- | Second || Nakamura Hiroken |- | Third || Nakamura Hiroko |- | Fourth || Nakamura Hirohiko |- | Fifth || Nakamura Kimiko |- | Sixth || Nakamura Hiroyuki |}
==Associated Shrines==
* {{ill|Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizugami Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=|1=Suijinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55522725}} (Tonomachi) - Registered as a religious corporation under the same name. * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine (Kawasaki)]] (Hinode) - Registered under the name Gōjima Shrine. * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine (Kawasaki City, Shiohama)]] (Shiohama) - Registered under the same name. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shimmei Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] (Kawanakashima and Shiohama).
==Access== * 3-minute walk from South Exit of [[:en:Kawasaki-Daishi Station|Kawasaki Daishi Station]] on the Keikyū Daishi Line. * Bus stop "Wakamiya Hachimangu" on the {{ill|Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|ja|川崎鶴見臨港バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinkō Bus|qq=|1=Kawasaki Tsurumi Rinko Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478611}} Kawasai 23 line.
